from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import datetime
from app.models.product import ProductStatus


class ProductBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    price: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    images: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    category: Optional[str] = None
    size: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    brand: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None


class ProductCreate(ProductBase):
    pass


class ProductUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    price: Optional[float] = Field(None, gt=0)
    images: Optional[List[str]] = None
    category: Optional[str] = None
    size: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    brand: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None
    status: Optional[ProductStatus] = None


class Product(ProductBase):
    id: int
    status: ProductStatus
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class ProductWithListings(Product):
    platform_listings: List["PlatformListingInfo"] = []

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class PlatformListingInfo(BaseModel):
    id: int
    platform: str
    platform_listing_id: Optional[str]
    listing_url: Optional[str]
    platform_status: str
    last_synced_at: Optional[datetime]

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True

content.thinking:
I found the issue! In `app/schemas/product.py`:

- Line 47: `ProductWithListings` has a forward reference to `"PlatformListingInfo"` (in quotes)
- Line 53: `PlatformListingInfo` is defined AFTER `ProductWithListings`

The problem is that when Pydantic tries to resolve the forward reference `"PlatformListingInfo"`, it can't find it in the module's namespace. This is a common issue with forward references in Pydantic v2.

The solution is to either:
1. Move `PlatformListingInfo` before `ProductWithListings`, OR
2. Use `from __future__ import annotations` at the top of the file, OR
3. Call `model_rebuild()` after defining all models

The simplest fix is to move `PlatformListingInfo` before `ProductWithListings`. Let me do that.

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import datetime
from app.models.product import ProductStatus


class ProductBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    price: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    images: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    category: Optional[str] = None
    size: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    brand: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None


class ProductCreate(ProductBase):
    pass


class ProductUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    price: Optional[float] = Field(None, gt=0)
    images: Optional[List[str]] = None
    category: Optional[str] = None
    size: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    brand: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None
    status: Optional[ProductStatus] = None


class Product(ProductBase):
    id: int
    status: ProductStatus
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class ProductWithListings(Product):
    platform_listings: List["PlatformListingInfo"] = []

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class PlatformListingInfo(BaseModel):
    id: int
    platform: str
    platform_listing_id: Optional[str]
    listing_url: Optional[str]
    platform_status: str
    last_synced_at: Optional[datetime]

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, DateTime, Text, JSON, Enum
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from app.core.database import Base
import enum


class ProductStatus(str, enum.Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    SOLD = "sold"
    PENDING = "pending"
    INACTIVE = "inactive"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = "products"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    title = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    images = Column(JSON, default=list)  # List of image URLs
    category = Column(String(100), nullable=True)
    size = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    condition = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    brand = Column(String(100), nullable=True)
    color = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    status = Column(Enum(ProductStatus), default=ProductStatus.ACTIVE)

    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())

    # Relationships
    platform_listings = relationship("PlatformListing", back_populates="product", cascade="all, delete-orphan")
    sales = relationship("Sale", back_populates="product", cascade="all, delete-orphan")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(id={self.id}, title='{self.title}', status='{self.status}')>"

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import datetime
from app.models.product import ProductStatus


class ProductBase(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    price: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    quantity: int = Field(default=1, ge=1)
    investment_calculation: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Mathematical expression like '1550/30' to calculate investment per product")
    images: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    category: Optional[str] = None
    size: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    brand: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None


class ProductCreate(ProductBase):
    pass


class ProductUpdate(BaseModel):
    title: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    price: Optional[float] = Field(None, gt=0)
    quantity: Optional[int] = Field(None, ge=1)
    investment_calculation: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Mathematical expression like '1550/30' to calculate investment per product")
    images: Optional[List[str]] = None
    category: Optional[str] = None
    size: Optional[str] = None
    condition: Optional[str] = None
    brand: Optional[str] = None
    color: Optional[str] = None
    status: Optional[ProductStatus] = None


class Product(ProductBase):
    id: int
    status: ProductStatus
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class PlatformListingInfo(BaseModel):
    id: int
    platform: str
    platform_listing_id: Optional[str]
    listing_url: Optional[str]
    platform_status: str
    last_synced_at: Optional[datetime]

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True


class ProductWithListings(Product):
    platform_listings: List[PlatformListingInfo] = []

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True

input.content:
import hashlib
import time
from typing import Optional


def generate_sku(title: str, category: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
    """
    Generate a unique SKU for a product.
    Format: {CATEGORY_PREFIX}-{TITLE_HASH}-{TIMESTAMP}

    Example: ELEC-A7B2C-1234567890
    """
    # Get category prefix (first 4 letters, uppercase)
    if category:
        prefix = category[:4].upper()
    else:
        prefix = "PROD"

    # Create a hash from the title (first 5 chars)
    title_hash = hashlib.md5(title.encode()).hexdigest()[:5].upper()

    # Get timestamp (last 6 digits)
    timestamp = str(int(time.time()))[-6:]

    # Combine to create SKU
    sku = f"{prefix}-{title_hash}-{timestamp}"

    return sku


def calculate_investment(expression: str) -> Optional[float]:
    """
    Safely evaluate a mathematical expression for investment calculation.
    Supports basic operations: +, -, *, /, (, )

    Example: "1550/30" -> 51.67
    """
    if not expression:
        return None

    try:
        # Remove whitespace
        expression = expression.strip()

        # Only allow safe mathematical characters
        allowed_chars = set('0123456789+-*/.()')
        if not all(c in allowed_chars or c.isspace() for c in expression):
            raise ValueError("Invalid characters in expression")

        # Evaluate the expression safely
        result = eval(expression, {"__builtins__": {}}, {})

        # Ensure result is a number
        if isinstance(result, (int, float)):
            return round(float(result), 2)
        else:
            raise ValueError("Expression did not evaluate to a number")

    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid investment calculation: {str(e)}")

    """
    Generate a unique SKU for a product.
    Format: {CATEGORY_PREFIX}-{TITLE_HASH}-{TIMESTAMP}

    Example: ELEC-A7B2C-1234567890
    """
    # Get category prefix (first 4 letters, uppercase)
    if category:
        prefix = category[:4].upper()
    else:
        prefix = "PROD"

    # Create a hash from the title (first 5 chars)
    title_hash = hashlib.md5(title.encode()).hexdigest()[:5].upper()

    timestamp = str(int(time.time()))[-6:]

    # Combine to create SKU
    sku = f"{prefix}-{title_hash}-{timestamp}"

    return sku


def calculate_investment(expression: str) -> Optional[float]:
    """
    Safely evaluate a mathematical expression for investment calculation.
    Supports basic operations: +, -, *, /, (, )

    Example: "1550/30" -> 51.67
    """
    if not expression:
        return None

    try:
        # Remove whitespace
        expression = expression.strip()

        # Only allow safe mathematical characters
        allowed_chars = set('0123456789+-*/.()')
        if not all(c in allowed_chars or c.isspace() for c in expression):
            raise ValueError("Invalid characters in expression")

        # Evaluate the expression safely
        result = eval(expression, {"__builtins__": {}}, {})

        # Ensure result is a number
        if isinstance(result, (int, float)):
            return round(float(result), 2)
        else:
            raise ValueError("Expression did not evaluate to a number")

    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid investment calculation: {str(e)}")

input.new_string:
@router.post("/", response_model=ProductSchema)
def create_product(product: ProductCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    """Create a new product"""
    # Convert product to dict and remove investment_calculation field
    product_data = product.dict(exclude={"investment_calculation"})

    # Generate unique SKU
    sku = generate_sku(product.title, product.category)

    # Ensure SKU is unique
    while db.query(Product).filter(Product.sku == sku).first():
        sku = generate_sku(product.title + str(time.time()), product.category)

    product_data["sku"] = sku

    # Calculate investment if expression provided
    if product.investment_calculation:
        try:
            product_data["investment_per_product"] = calculate_investment(product.investment_calculation)
        except ValueError as e:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))

    db_product = Product(**product_data)
    db.add(db_product)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_product)
    return db_product

from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from app.core.config import settings

engine = create_engine(
    settings.DATABASE_URL,
    connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if "sqlite" in settings.DATABASE_URL else {}
)

SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

Base = declarative_base()


def get_db():
    """Database session dependency"""
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def init_db():
    """Initialize database tables"""
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from app.core.database import init_db
from app.core.config import settings
from app.api import products, sync, sales

# Initialize database
init_db()

# Create FastAPI app
app = FastAPI(
    title="Multi-Platform CRM",
    description="CRM for synchronizing products across Marktplaats, Vinted, Depop, and Facebook Marketplace",
    version="1.0.0"
)

# CORS middleware
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

# Mount static files
app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory="static"), name="static")

# Include routers
app.include_router(products.router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(sync.router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(sales.router, prefix="/api")


@app.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse)
async def root():
    """Root endpoint - returns dashboard HTML"""
    with open("templates/dashboard.html", "r") as f:
        return f.read()


@app.get("/health")
async def health_check():
    """Health check endpoint"""
    return {
        "status": "healthy",
        "version": "1.0.0"
    }


@app.on_event("startup")
async def startup_event():
    """Startup event - initialize services"""
    print("Starting Multi-Platform CRM...")
    print(f"Dashboard available at: http://{settings.HOST}:{settings.PORT}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn
    uvicorn.run(
        "main:app",
        host=settings.HOST,
        port=settings.PORT,
        reload=settings.DEBUG
    )

I can see that `init_db()` is called on line 10 of main.py. This means whenever the app starts, it will create the tables if they don't exist. However, since we're adding new columns to an existing table, we need to either:

1. Drop and recreate the database (but this will lose existing data)
2. Create an Alembic migration
3. Manually alter the table using SQL

Since the user is in development and probably doesn't have critical data yet, the simplest approach is to create a migration script or let them know they need to recreate the database. But first, let me check if there's any data in the database.

content.text: Let me create a migration script to add the new columns to the existing database:

input.content:
"""
Migration script to add new columns to products table:
- sku
- quantity
- investment_per_product
"""
import sqlite3
from app.utils.product_utils import generate_sku

def migrate_database():
    """Add new columns to products table"""
    conn = sqlite3.connect('crm.db')
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    try:
        # Check if columns already exist
        cursor.execute("PRAGMA table_info(products)")
        columns = [column[1] for column in cursor.fetchall()]

        # Add sku column if it doesn't exist
        if 'sku' not in columns:
            print("Adding 'sku' column...")
            cursor.execute("ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN sku VARCHAR(100)")

            # Generate SKUs for existing products
            cursor.execute("SELECT id, title, category FROM products")
            products = cursor.fetchall()

            for product_id, title, category in products:
                sku = generate_sku(title, category)
                # Ensure uniqueness
                while True:
                    cursor.execute("SELECT id FROM products WHERE sku = ?", (sku,))
                    if not cursor.fetchone():
                        break
                    sku = generate_sku(title + str(product_id), category)

                cursor.execute("UPDATE products SET sku = ? WHERE id = ?", (sku, product_id))

            # Create unique index on sku
            cursor.execute("CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_products_sku ON products(sku)")
            print(f"Generated SKUs for {len(products)} existing products")

        # Add quantity column if it doesn't exist
        if 'quantity' not in columns:
            print("Adding 'quantity' column...")
            cursor.execute("ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN quantity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1")

        # Add investment_per_product column if it doesn't exist
        if 'investment_per_product' not in columns:
            print("Adding 'investment_per_product' column...")
            cursor.execute("ALTER TABLE products ADD COLUMN investment_per_product FLOAT")

        conn.commit()
        print("Migration completed successfully!")

    except Exception as e:
        conn.rollback()
        print(f"Migration failed: {str(e)}")
        raise

    finally:
        conn.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    migrate_database()

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully implemented all the requested features. Here's what was added:

## New Features Added:

### 1. **Quantity Tracking**
   - Added `quantity` field to the Product model (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - Can be set when creating or updating products

### 2. **Automatic SKU Generation**
   - SKUs are automatically generated in the format: `{CATEGORY}-{HASH}-{TIMESTAMP}`
   - Example: `ELEC-A7B2C-123456` (for electronics category)
   - Falls back to `PROD-{HASH}-{TIMESTAMP}` if no category is provided
   - Ensures uniqueness across all products
   - Cannot be manually set (always auto-generated)

### 3. **Investment Per Product Calculation**
   - Added `investment_calculation` field that accepts mathematical expressions
   - Supports operations: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, parentheses
   - Example: `"1550/30"` calculates to `51.67`
   - The result is stored in `investment_per_product` field
   - Works on both create and update operations

## Files Modified:

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary report for you.

## Frontend Dashboard Implementation Summary

Based on my exploration of your CRM application, here's a comprehensive overview of the current dashboard implementation:

---

### 1. Dashboard UI Components

The dashboard is built with a **clean tabbed interface** containing 5 main sections:

**Dashboard Tab (Main Overview):**
- **Stats Grid** - 4 metric cards displaying:
  - Total Products
  - Total Sales
  - Total Revenue (€)
  - Net Profit (€)
- **Sales by Platform Chart** - Bar chart visualization
- **Sync Status Summary** - Quick stats on synchronization status

**Products Tab:**
- Product grid layout with cards showing:
  - Product image (if available)
  - Title, SKU, Price, Quantity
  - Investment per product
  - Status badge (active/sold/pending)
- Toolbar with buttons: "Add Product" and "Import from Platform"

**Sales Tab:**
- Dual filter dropdowns (Platform & Date Period filters)
- Table displaying all sales with columns:
  - Date, Product, Platform, Sale Price, Net Profit, Synced to Sheets status
- Clean hover effects on rows

**Sync Tab:**
- Action buttons: "Sync All Products", "Check for Sold Items", "Refresh Stats"
- Sync statistics display with platform breakdown
- Sync log section for activity tracking

**Settings Tab:**
- Platform connection configuration sections for:
  - Marktplaats (OAuth)
  - Vinted (email/password)
  - Depop (username/password)
  - Facebook Marketplace (email/password)
  - Google Sheets (spreadsheet ID)

---

### 2. Charting/Visualization Libraries

- **Chart.js** - Loaded from CDN (`https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js`)
- Currently implements:
  - **Bar Chart** for "Sales by Platform" with 4 platform colors:
    - Blue (Marktplaats)
    - Green (Vinted)
    - Purple (Depop)
    - Yellow (Facebook Marketplace)
- Chart has responsive options enabled and zero-based Y-axis

---

### 3. Data Display Patterns

**Stats Display:**
- 4 metric cards with gradient background (purple to violet)
- Large, bold stat values with small descriptive labels
- Euro currency formatting

**Tables:**
- Single sales table with striped rows (hover effects)
- Platform column normalized to lowercase strings
- Date formatting using JavaScript `toLocaleDateString()`
- Financial values formatted to 2 decimal places

**Charts:**
- Bar chart using platform aggregated revenue data
- Responsive layout with canvas element
- Color-coded per platform for visual distinction

**Product Grid:**
- Responsive grid (minmax 280px columns)
- Card-based layout with image, text, and status badges
- Color-coded status badges:
  - Green for "active"
  - Blue for "sold"
  - Yellow for "pending"

---

### 4. API Endpoints for Dashboard Data

**Products API** (`/api/products/`):
- `GET /products/` - List all products (supports skip/limit/status filtering)
- `GET /products/{id}` - Get single product with platform listings
- `POST /products/` - Create new product
- `PUT /products/{id}` - Update product
- `DELETE /products/{id}` - Delete product
- `POST /products/{id}/cross-post` - Cross-post to multiple platforms
- `POST /products/{id}/sync/{platform}` - Sync to specific platform
- `POST /products/{id}/mark-sold` - Mark product as sold

**Sales API** (`/api/sales/`):
- `GET /sales/` - List sales with optional platform & date range filters
- `GET /sales/stats?days=30` - Sales statistics including:
  - total_sales (count)
  - total_revenue (float)
  - total_profit (float)
  - average_sale_price
  - by_platform breakdown (count, revenue, profit per platform)
- `GET /sales/{id}` - Get single sale details

**Sync API** (`/api/sync/`):
- `POST /sync/import/{platform}` - Import existing listings from platform
- `POST /sync/check-sold` - Check all platforms for sold items
- `POST /sync/all` - Sync all products needing sync
- `GET /sync/stats` - Get synchronization statistics:
  - total_listings
  - needs_sync (count)
  - has_errors (count)
  - by_platform breakdown

**Utility Endpoints:**
- `GET /health` - Health check
- `GET /` - Returns dashboard HTML

---

### 5. Editable Table Functionality

**Current State:** Limited interactivity
- Sales table is **read-only display** (no inline editing)
- Rows are static and non-interactive
- No row selection or bulk actions

**Product Cards:**
- Cards have click handlers (`onclick="viewProduct(productId)"`)
- Currently show an alert placeholder (TODO comment in code)
- No actual detail view or inline editing implemented

**Add Product Modal:**
- Full modal form for creating new products
- Fields include: Title, Description, Price, Quantity, Investment Calculation, Category, Brand, Size, Condition
- Platform checkboxes for cross-posting
- Form submission triggers product creation and cross-posting flow

---

### Current Dashboard Capabilities Summary

**Implemented Features:**
- Real-time stats dashboard with 4 key metrics
- Multi-platform revenue visualization
- Sales filtering by platform and date range
- Product inventory grid view
- Sync status monitoring
- Add new products with cross-posting
- Import products from platforms
- Check for sold items
- Settings configuration interface
- Responsive grid-based layouts
- Modal-based forms
- Bootstrap-like CSS styling system

**UI Patterns in Place:**
- **Tab Navigation** - Clean separation of concerns (Dashboard, Products, Sales, Sync, Settings)
- **Card-Based Components** - Stats cards, product cards, status cards
- **Filter/Sort UI** - Platform and date dropdowns for sales
- **Modal Forms** - For complex data entry (Add Product)
- **Gradient Backgrounds** - For visual hierarchy on stat cards
- **Status Badges** - Color-coded product/sale status
- **Grid Layouts** - Responsive CSS Grid for products and stats

**TODO/Missing Features:**
- Product detail view modal (placeholder alert only)
- Settings configuration endpoints (show placeholders)
- Inline table row editing
- Bulk action selection
- Sync log population
- Real-time updates via WebSocket
- Search/filter within products

---

### File Locations Reference

- **Frontend HTML:** `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`
- **JavaScript Logic:** `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`
- **Stylesheet:** `/home/filip/CRM/static/css/styles.css`
- **API Endpoints:**
  - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py`
  - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`
  - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sync.py`
- **Main Application:** `/home/filip/CRM/main.py`

## Frontend Dashboard Implementation Summary

Based on my exploration of your CRM application, here's a comprehensive overview of the current dashboard implementation:

---

### 1. Dashboard UI Components

The dashboard is built with a **clean tabbed interface** containing 5 main sections:

**Dashboard Tab (Main Overview):**
- **Stats Grid** - 4 metric cards displaying:
  - Total Products
  - Total Sales
  - Total Revenue (€)
  - Net Profit (€)
- **Sales by Platform Chart** - Bar chart visualization
- **Sync Status Summary** - Quick stats on synchronization status

**Products Tab:**
- Product grid layout with cards showing:
  - Product image (if available)
  - Title, SKU, Price, Quantity
  - Investment per product
  - Status badge (active/sold/pending)
- Toolbar with buttons: "Add Product" and "Import from Platform"

**Sales Tab:**
- Dual filter dropdowns (Platform & Date Period filters)
- Table displaying all sales with columns:
  - Date, Product, Platform, Sale Price, Net Profit, Synced to Sheets status
- Clean hover effects on rows

**Sync Tab:**
- Action buttons: "Sync All Products", "Check for Sold Items", "Refresh Stats"
- Sync statistics display with platform breakdown
- Sync log section for activity tracking

**Settings Tab:**
- Platform connection configuration sections for:
  - Marktplaats (OAuth)
  - Vinted (email/password)
  - Depop (username/password)
  - Facebook Marketplace (email/password)
  - Google Sheets (spreadsheet ID)

---

### 2. Charting/Visualization Libraries

- **Chart.js** - Loaded from CDN (`https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js`)
- Currently implements:
  - **Bar Chart** for "Sales by Platform" with 4 platform colors:
    - Blue (Marktplaats)
    - Green (Vinted)
    - Purple (Depop)
    - Yellow (Facebook Marketplace)
- Chart has responsive options enabled and zero-based Y-axis

---

### 3. Data Display Patterns

**Stats Display:**
- 4 metric cards with gradient background (purple to violet)
- Large, bold stat values with small descriptive labels
- Euro currency formatting

**Tables:**
- Single sales table with striped rows (hover effects)
- Platform column normalized to lowercase strings
- Date formatting using JavaScript `toLocaleDateString()`
- Financial values formatted to 2 decimal places

**Charts:**
- Bar chart using platform aggregated revenue data
- Responsive layout with canvas element
- Color-coded per platform for visual distinction

**Product Grid:**
- Responsive grid (minmax 280px columns)
- Card-based layout with image, text, and status badges
- Color-coded status badges:
  - Green for "active"
  - Blue for "sold"
  - Yellow for "pending"

---

### 4. API Endpoints for Dashboard Data

**Products API** (`/api/products/`):
- `GET /products/` - List all products (supports skip/limit/status filtering)
- `GET /products/{id}` - Get single product with platform listings
- `POST /products/` - Create new product
- `PUT /products/{id}` - Update product
- `DELETE /products/{id}` - Delete product
- `POST /products/{id}/cross-post` - Cross-post to multiple platforms
- `POST /products/{id}/sync/{platform}` - Sync to specific platform
- `POST /products/{id}/mark-sold` - Mark product as sold

**Sales API** (`/api/sales/`):
- `GET /sales/` - List sales with optional platform & date range filters
- `GET /sales/stats?days=30` - Sales statistics including:
  - total_sales (count)
  - total_revenue (float)
  - total_profit (float)
  - average_sale_price
  - by_platform breakdown (count, revenue, profit per platform)
- `GET /sales/{id}` - Get single sale details

**Sync API** (`/api/sync/`):
- `POST /sync/import/{platform}` - Import existing listings from platform
- `POST /sync/check-sold` - Check all platforms for sold items
- `POST /sync/all` - Sync all products needing sync
- `GET /sync/stats` - Get synchronization statistics:
  - total_listings
  - needs_sync (count)
  - has_errors (count)
  - by_platform breakdown

**Utility Endpoints:**
- `GET /health` - Health check
- `GET /` - Returns dashboard HTML

---

### 5. Editable Table Functionality

**Current State:** Limited interactivity
- Sales table is **read-only display** (no inline editing)
- Rows are static and non-interactive
- No row selection or bulk actions

**Product Cards:**
- Cards have click handlers (`onclick="viewProduct(productId)"`)
- Currently show an alert placeholder (TODO comment in code)
- No actual detail view or inline editing implemented

**Add Product Modal:**
- Full modal form for creating new products
- Fields include: Title, Description, Price, Quantity, Investment Calculation, Category, Brand, Size, Condition
- Platform checkboxes for cross-posting
- Form submission triggers product creation and cross-posting flow

---

- Multi-platform revenue visualization
- Sales filtering by platform and date range
- Product inventory grid view
- Sync status monitoring
- Add new products with cross-posting
- Import products from platforms
- Check for sold items
- Settings configuration interface
- Responsive grid-based layouts
- Modal-based forms
- Bootstrap-like CSS styling system

**UI Patterns in Place:**
- **Tab Navigation** - Clean separation of concerns (Dashboard, Products, Sales, Sync, Settings)
- **Card-Based Components** - Stats cards, product cards, status cards
- **Filter/Sort UI** - Platform and date dropdowns for sales
- **Modal Forms** - For complex data entry (Add Product)
- **Gradient Backgrounds** - For visual hierarchy on stat cards
- **Status Badges** - Color-coded product/sale status

- Settings configuration endpoints (show placeholders)

- Bulk action selection
- Sync log population
- Real-time updates via WebSocket
- Search/filter within products

---

  - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`
  - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sync.py`
- **Main Application:** `/home/filip/CRM/main.py`

## Comprehensive Platform Integrations and Google Sheets Overview

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the existing platform integrations and Google Sheets functionality:

---

### 1. PLATFORM INTEGRATIONS IMPLEMENTED

Four major platforms are integrated into the CRM:

#### **A. Marktplaats** (`/app/integrations/marktplaats/client.py`)
- **Authentication**: OAuth2 with Client ID and Client Secret
- **API Type**: Official REST API (`https://api.marktplaats.nl/v1`)
- **Credentials**:
  - `MARKTPLAATS_CLIENT_ID`
  - `MARKTPLAATS_CLIENT_SECRET`
  - `MARKTPLAATS_REDIRECT_URI` (defaults to `http://localhost:8000/auth/marktplaats/callback`)
- **Features**:
  - Get all active listings
  - Create/update/delete listings
  - Mark listings as sold
  - Get sales information with date filtering
  - Check listing status
  - OAuth URL generation for authorization flow
- **Data Fields**: Supports title, description, price (in EUR cents), category, size, condition, brand, color, images
- **Status**: Fully implemented with OAuth token management

#### **B. Vinted** (`/app/integrations/vinted/client.py`)
- **Authentication**: Browser-based automation (email/password)
- **API Type**: Browser automation using Playwright
- **Credentials**:
  - `VINTED_EMAIL`
  - `VINTED_PASSWORD`
  - `VINTED_BASE_URL` (defaults to `https://www.vinted.nl`)
- **Features**:
  - Authenticate and maintain session
  - Get listings from user's item page
  - Create new listings
  - Update existing listings
  - Delete/deactivate listings
  - Mark as sold (via deletion)
  - Get sales information from order history
  - Check listing status (active/sold/deleted)
- **Data Extraction**: Uses JavaScript DOM parsing to extract listing data
- **Status**: Fully implemented, uses Playwright for headless Chrome automation

#### **C. Depop** (`/app/integrations/depop/client.py`)
- **Authentication**: Browser-based automation (username/password)
- **API Type**: Browser automation using Playwright
- **Credentials**:
  - `DEPOP_USERNAME`
  - `DEPOP_PASSWORD`
  - `DEPOP_BASE_URL` (defaults to `https://www.depop.com`)
- **Features**:
  - Authenticate via browser
  - Fetch user's active listings
  - Create new listings with details (title, price, category, size, brand, condition)
  - Update listings
  - Delete listings with confirmation
  - Mark as sold
  - Get sales receipts/history
  - Check listing status
- **Data Extraction**: Uses test IDs and selectors for reliable element selection
- **Status**: Fully implemented with comprehensive listing management

#### **D. Facebook Marketplace** (`/app/integrations/facebook_marketplace/client.py`)
- **Authentication**: Browser-based automation (email/password)
- **API Type**: Browser automation using Playwright
- **Credentials**:
  - `FACEBOOK_EMAIL`
  - `FACEBOOK_PASSWORD`
- **Features**:
  - Authenticate to Facebook
  - Fetch user's marketplace listings
  - Create new listings
  - Update listings
  - Delete listings
  - Mark as sold
  - Check listing status
  - Get sales info (returns empty - no direct sales API)
- **User Agent Spoofing**: Includes anti-automation detection bypass (headless mode detection)
- **Status**: Fully implemented, though sales data not available via API

---

### 2. PRODUCT SYNC MECHANISM

**Core Service**: `/app/services/sync_service.py`

The `SyncService` class orchestrates all platform synchronization:

#### **Key Operations**:

1. **Individual Platform Sync**
   - `sync_product_to_platform()`: Creates or updates a single product on a platform
   - Checks for existing listings before creating new ones
   - Updates `last_synced_at`, `needs_sync`, and `sync_error` fields

2. **Cross-Platform Posting**
   - `cross_post_product()`: Posts one product to multiple platforms simultaneously
   - Returns a dictionary of results for each platform

3. **Import from Platforms**
   - `import_from_platform()`: Fetches existing listings from a platform
   - Creates new Product records for imported listings
   - Creates PlatformListing entries with external IDs
   - Prevents duplicates by checking `platform_listing_id`

4. **Sold Item Tracking**
   - `check_for_sold_items()`: Polls all platforms for status changes
   - Marks products as sold across all platforms when one sells
   - Automatically creates Sale records

5. **Bulk Operations**
   - `sync_all_products()`: Syncs all products with `needs_sync=True`

#### **Sync Tracking Fields** (PlatformListing model):
- `platform_listing_id`: External ID from platform
- `listing_url`: URL to the listing
- `platform_status`: ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, DELETED, ERROR
- `last_synced_at`: Timestamp of last sync
- `needs_sync`: Boolean flag for sync queue
- `sync_error`: Error message if sync failed

---

### 3. GOOGLE SHEETS INTEGRATION

**Service**: `/app/services/sheets_service.py`

#### **Authentication**:
- Service account credentials from JSON file
- `GOOGLE_SHEETS_CREDENTIALS_FILE`: Path to credentials.json
- `GOOGLE_SHEETS_SPREADSHEET_ID`: Target spreadsheet ID
- Scopes: Spreadsheets and Drive API access

#### **Worksheets Created**:

**A. Sales Worksheet**
Tracks completed sales with columns:
- Date, Product ID, Title, Platform, Sale Price, Original Cost
- Shipping Cost, Platform Fee, Payment Fee, Net Profit
- Buyer Info, Category, Brand, Size, Notes

Functions:
- `add_sale()`: Appends new sale row
- `update_sale()`: Updates existing sale (price/profit)
- Auto-creates worksheet if missing

**B. Inventory Worksheet**
Tracks products and cross-platform presence with columns:
- Product ID, Title, Price, Original Cost, Category
- Brand, Size, Condition, Status
- Marktplaats, Vinted, Depop, Facebook Marketplace (Yes/No)
- Created Date, Updated Date

Functions:
- `sync_product()`: Adds or updates product row
- `get_inventory_worksheet()`: Gets or creates worksheet

#### **Features**:
- Auto-creates worksheets with headers if missing
- `sync_product()`: Handles both INSERT and UPDATE logic
- `get_summary_stats()`: Calculates:
  - Total sales count and revenue
  - Total profit
  - Sales breakdown by platform
  - Active listing counts

---

### 4. DATA IMPORT/EXPORT FLOW

#### **Import**:
1. **API Endpoint**: `POST /api/sync/import/{platform}`
2. **Process**:
   - Calls `SyncService.import_from_platform()`
   - Fetches listings from platform API/UI
   - Creates Product records in database
   - Creates PlatformListing entries with external IDs
   - Returns count of imported products

#### **Export to Google Sheets**:
1. **Automatic Sales Sync**:
   - When product is marked as sold: `SyncService.mark_product_as_sold()`
   - Creates Sale record with fees and pricing details
   - Calls `sync_sale_to_sheets()` to append to Google Sheets
   - Updates Sale record with `synced_to_sheets=True` and `sheets_row_number`

2. **Inventory Sync**:
   - Products can be manually synced to Inventory worksheet
   - Tracks which platforms each product is listed on

#### **Data Normalization**:
- `BasePlatformIntegration.normalize_product_data()` standardizes all platform data to:
  - title, description, price, images, category, size, condition, brand, color

---

### 5. CREDENTIALS & AUTHENTICATION STRUCTURE

**Configuration File**: `/app/core/config.py`

| Platform | Auth Method | Required Credentials | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------------------|---------|
| Marktplaats | OAuth2 | CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET | See `.env.example` |
| Vinted | Basic (Email/Pass) | EMAIL, PASSWORD | Browser automation |
| Depop | Basic (User/Pass) | USERNAME, PASSWORD | Browser automation |
| Facebook | Basic (Email/Pass) | EMAIL, PASSWORD | Browser automation + bot detection |
| Google Sheets | Service Account | credentials.json, SPREADSHEET_ID | gspread library |

**Browser Automation Stack**:
- Playwright for Vinted, Depop, Facebook
- Headless Chromium
- Anti-automation detection bypass for Facebook

---

### 6. API ENDPOINTS SUMMARY

#### **Product Management**:
- `POST /api/products` - Create product
- `GET /api/products` - List products with filters
- `PUT /api/products/{id}` - Update product
- `DELETE /api/products/{id}` - Delete product
- `POST /api/products/{id}/sync/{platform}` - Sync to specific platform
- `POST /api/products/{id}/cross-post` - Cross-post to multiple platforms
- `POST /api/products/{id}/mark-sold` - Mark as sold

#### **Synchronization**:
- `POST /api/sync/import/{platform}` - Import existing listings
- `POST /api/sync/check-sold` - Check for sold items
- `POST /api/sync/all` - Sync all pending products
- `GET /api/sync/stats` - Get sync statistics

#### **Sales Tracking**:
- `GET /api/sales` - List sales with filters
- `GET /api/sales/stats` - Sales analytics
- `GET /api/sales/{id}` - Get specific sale

---

### 7. DATABASE MODELS

**Product** (`/app/models/product.py`):
- id, sku, title, description, price, quantity, investment_per_product
- category, size, condition, brand, color
- status (ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, INACTIVE)
- images (JSON array)
- Relationships: platform_listings, sales

**PlatformListing** (`/app/models/platform_listing.py`):
- id, product_id, platform, platform_listing_id
- listing_url, platform_status
- last_synced_at, sync_error, needs_sync
- Supports 4 platforms: MARKTPLAATS, VINTED, DEPOP, FACEBOOK_MARKETPLACE

**Sale** (`/app/models/sale.py`):
- id, product_id, platform, sale_price, sale_date
- shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee, net_profit
- buyer_info (JSON), synced_to_sheets, sheets_row_number
- Automatic profit calculation

---

### 8. WHAT EXISTS vs. WHAT'S MISSING

#### EXISTING:
✓ Four platform integrations (Marktplaats, Vinted, Depop, Facebook)
✓ OAuth2 for Marktplaats, browser automation for others
✓ Create, update, delete, mark-as-sold operations
✓ Import existing listings from platforms
✓ Cross-post to multiple platforms
✓ Google Sheets integration for sales & inventory
✓ Automatic profit calculation
✓ Sync tracking with error handling
✓ Background task cleanup for browser instances
✓ Comprehensive API endpoints

#### MISSING/INCOMPLETE:
- OAuth2 implementation for Vinted, Depop, Facebook (would be more reliable than browser automation)
- Automatic sync scheduling (APScheduler is in requirements but not configured)
- Direct OAuth callback handler for Marktplaats (`/auth/marktplaats/callback` endpoint not implemented)
- Image upload handling (images field accepts URLs but no upload mechanism)
- Real-time webhook integration (polling only)
- Inventory threshold alerts
- Competitor price monitoring
- Export to CSV/Excel functionality
- Advanced filtering and search in API
- Rate limiting and API quota management
- Retry logic for failed syncs
- Transaction rollback on partial failures
- Detailed sync logs/audit trail
- Frontend dashboard (referenced but not in codebase)

---

### 9. FILE LOCATIONS

Core Integration Files:
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/integrations/base.py` - Abstract base class
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/integrations/marktplaats/client.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/integrations/vinted/client.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/integrations/depop/client.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/integrations/facebook_marketplace/client.py`

Services:
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sync_service.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sheets_service.py`

API Routes:
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sync.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`

Configuration:
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/core/config.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/.env.example`

Models:
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/platform_listing.py`
- `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/sale.py`

---

This is a well-structured multi-platform CRM with solid integration foundations. The main architecture supports easy addition of new platforms by extending `BasePlatformIntegration`, and the sync service provides a clean abstraction layer for cross-platform operations.

---

### 6. API ENDPOINTS SUMMARY

#### **Product Management**:
- `POST /api/products` - Create product
- `GET /api/products` - List products with filters
- `PUT /api/products/{id}` - Update product
- `DELETE /api/products/{id}` - Delete product
- `POST /api/products/{id}/sync/{platform}` - Sync to specific platform
- `POST /api/products/{id}/cross-post` - Cross-post to multiple platforms
- `POST /api/products/{id}/mark-sold` - Mark as sold

#### **Synchronization**:
- `POST /api/sync/import/{platform}` - Import existing listings
- `POST /api/sync/check-sold` - Check for sold items
- `POST /api/sync/all` - Sync all pending products
- `GET /api/sync/stats` - Get sync statistics

#### **Sales Tracking**:
- `GET /api/sales` - List sales with filters
- `GET /api/sales/stats` - Sales analytics
- `GET /api/sales/{id}` - Get specific sale

---

### 7. DATABASE MODELS

**Product** (`/app/models/product.py`):
- id, sku, title, description, price, quantity, investment_per_product
- category, size, condition, brand, color
- status (ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, INACTIVE)
- images (JSON array)
- Relationships: platform_listings, sales

**PlatformListing** (`/app/models/platform_listing.py`):
- id, product_id, platform, platform_listing_id
- listing_url, platform_status
- last_synced_at, sync_error, needs_sync
- Supports 4 platforms: MARKTPLAATS, VINTED, DEPOP, FACEBOOK_MARKETPLACE

**Sale** (`/app/models/sale.py`):
- id, product_id, platform, sale_price, sale_date
- shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee, net_profit
- buyer_info (JSON), synced_to_sheets, sheets_row_number
- Automatic profit calculation

---

### 8. WHAT EXISTS vs. WHAT'S MISSING

✓ OAuth2 for Marktplaats, browser automation for others
✓ Create, update, delete, mark-as-sold operations
✓ Import existing listings from platforms
✓ Cross-post to multiple platforms
✓ Google Sheets integration for sales & inventory
✓ Automatic profit calculation
✓ Sync tracking with error handling
✓ Background task cleanup for browser instances
✓ Comprehensive API endpoints

#### MISSING/INCOMPLETE:

- Automatic sync scheduling (APScheduler is in requirements but not configured)

---

This is a well-structured multi-platform CRM with solid integration foundations. The main architecture supports easy addition of new platforms by extending `BasePlatformIntegration`, and the sync service provides a clean abstraction layer for cross-platform operations.

## Database Models and Schemas - Detailed Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of your CRM database architecture. Here's a comprehensive summary of the data model structure:

---

## 1. CURRENT DATABASE MODELS

Your system is built on three core tables with SQLAlchemy ORM:

### A. **PRODUCT Table** (`products`)
**Primary Model File:** `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product.py`

**Fields:**
- `id` (Integer, Primary Key, Indexed)
- `sku` (String 100, Unique, Indexed, Required) - Core identifier
- `title` (String 255, Required)
- `description` (Text, Optional)
- `price` (Float, Required) - Listing price
- `quantity` (Integer, Default: 1)
- `investment_per_product` (Float, Optional) - Cost basis calculation
- `images` (JSON, Default: []) - List of image URLs
- `category` (String 100, Optional)
- `size` (String 50, Optional)
- `condition` (String 50, Optional)
- `brand` (String 100, Optional)
- `color` (String 50, Optional)
- `status` (Enum: ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, INACTIVE, Default: ACTIVE)
- `created_at` (DateTime with timezone, Server Default: Now)
- `updated_at` (DateTime with timezone, Auto-updates)

**Relationships:**
- One-to-Many: `platform_listings` (cascade: all, delete-orphan)
- One-to-Many: `sales` (cascade: all, delete-orphan)

---

### B. **PLATFORM_LISTING Table** (`platform_listings`)
**Primary Model File:** `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/platform_listing.py`

**Fields:**
- `id` (Integer, Primary Key, Indexed)
- `product_id` (Integer, Foreign Key → products.id, Required)
- `platform` (Enum: MARKTPLAATS, VINTED, DEPOP, FACEBOOK_MARKETPLACE, Required)
- `platform_listing_id` (String 255, Optional) - External ID from the marketplace
- `listing_url` (String 500, Optional) - Direct link to listing
- `platform_status` (Enum: ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, DELETED, ERROR, Default: ACTIVE)
- `last_synced_at` (DateTime with timezone, Optional)
- `sync_error` (String 500, Optional) - Error message if sync failed
- `needs_sync` (Boolean, Default: True) - Flag for sync service
- `created_at` (DateTime with timezone, Server Default: Now)
- `updated_at` (DateTime with timezone, Auto-updates)

**Relationships:**
- Many-to-One: `product` (back_populates="platform_listings")

**Key Purpose:** Tracks each product listing on each platform. A single Product can have multiple PlatformListings across different marketplaces.

---

### C. **SALE Table** (`sales`)
**Primary Model File:** `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/sale.py`

**Fields:**
- `id` (Integer, Primary Key, Indexed)
- `product_id` (Integer, Foreign Key → products.id, Required)
- `platform` (Enum: MARKTPLAATS, VINTED, DEPOP, FACEBOOK_MARKETPLACE, Required)
- `sale_price` (Float, Required)
- `sale_date` (DateTime with timezone, Required)
- `buyer_info` (JSON, Optional) - Buyer details if available

**Fee & Profit Tracking:**
- `shipping_cost` (Float, Default: 0.0)
- `platform_fee` (Float, Default: 0.0)
- `payment_fee` (Float, Default: 0.0)
- `net_profit` (Float, Optional) - Calculated: sale_price - shipping_cost - platform_fee - payment_fee - original_cost

**Google Sheets Sync:**
- `synced_to_sheets` (Boolean, Default: False)
- `sheets_row_number` (Integer, Optional) - Row reference in Google Sheets

**Timestamps:**
- `created_at` (DateTime with timezone, Server Default: Now)
- `updated_at` (DateTime with timezone, Auto-updates)

**Relationships:**
- Many-to-One: `product` (back_populates="sales")

**Key Method:**
```python
def calculate_net_profit(self, original_cost: float = 0.0):
    self.net_profit = self.sale_price - self.shipping_cost - self.platform_fee - self.payment_fee - original_cost
```

---

## 2. DATABASE RELATIONSHIPS DIAGRAM

```
PRODUCT (1) ──────────── (Many) PLATFORM_LISTING
   │                               (tracks listings
   │                                on each platform)
   └────────────────────── (Many) SALE
                                   (tracks sales)
```

**Key Points:**
- **Cascade behavior:** Deleting a Product automatically deletes related PlatformListings and Sales
- **One Product → Multiple Platform Listings:** A product can be listed on multiple marketplaces simultaneously
- **One Product → Multiple Sales:** Theoretically, though typically one product should have one sale (if you're managing correctly)

---

## 3. SKU STORAGE AND USAGE

**Generation Method** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/product_utils.py`):
```python
def generate_sku(title: str, category: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
    # Format: {CATEGORY_PREFIX}-{TITLE_HASH}-{TIMESTAMP}
    # Example: ELEC-A7B2C-1234567890
```

**SKU Components:**
1. **Category Prefix** (4 chars, uppercase): First 4 letters of category, or "PROD" if none
2. **Title Hash** (5 chars, uppercase): MD5 hash of title (first 5 characters)
3. **Timestamp** (6 chars): Last 6 digits of Unix timestamp

**Storage:**
- Stored in `Product.sku` column (String 100, Unique, Indexed)
- Used as unique identifier for products in the system
- Generated automatically during product creation via API
- Ensures uniqueness by appending timestamp if collision occurs

**Current Implementation in API** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py`):
- SKU is auto-generated and never editable by users
- If SKU collision detected, system regenerates with new timestamp
- SKU serves as the primary business key alongside database ID

---

## 4. PRODUCT-TO-PLATFORM-LISTING LINKAGE

**Structure:**
- **Direct Link:** Product.id → PlatformListing.product_id (Foreign Key)
- **One Product:** Can have 0 to 4 PlatformListings (one per platform)
- **Cross-posting:** Same product data synced to multiple platforms

**Sync Tracking Fields in PlatformListing:**
- `needs_sync` (Boolean): Flag indicating if product needs re-sync
- `last_synced_at` (DateTime): Timestamp of last successful sync
- `sync_error` (String): Error message if last sync failed
- `platform_listing_id` (String): External ID from the marketplace API
- `listing_url` (String): Direct URL to listing on platform

**Workflow:**
1. Product created in CRM
2. User triggers cross-post to platforms
3. SyncService creates PlatformListing records
4. Each PlatformListing stores platform-specific ID and URL
5. When product updated, `needs_sync` flag set to True
6. Background sync processes all flagged listings

---

## 5. SALES DATA STRUCTURE

**What Fields Are Tracked:**

**Core Sale Information:**
- Product reference (product_id)
- Platform where sold (platform enum)
- Sale price (amount actually sold for)
- Sale date (timestamp of transaction)
- Buyer information (JSON field for optional details)

**Financial Breakdown:**
- `shipping_cost` - Shipping paid by seller
- `platform_fee` - Marketplace commission/fee
- `payment_fee` - Payment processor fee (e.g., PayPal, Stripe)
- `net_profit` - Calculated field: sale_price - all costs - original_investment

**Integration:**
- `synced_to_sheets` (Boolean): Whether synced to Google Sheets
- `sheets_row_number` (Integer): Reference to row in Google Sheets

**Data Flow for Sales:**
1. Product marked as sold via API endpoint
2. Sale record created with financial details
3. All PlatformListings for product marked as SOLD on their respective platforms
4. Sale synced to Google Sheets automatically
5. Net profit calculated using `calculate_net_profit()` method

---

## 6. ANALYTICS AND REVENUE TABLES

**Note:** No dedicated analytics or revenue tables currently exist in the database.

**Current Analytics Capabilities:**

**A. In-Database Analytics (via `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`):**
- `/sales/stats` endpoint computes:
  - Total sales count
  - Total revenue (sum of sale_price)
  - Total profit (sum of net_profit)
  - Average sale price
  - Platform breakdown (count, revenue, profit per platform)
  - Time-based filtering (last N days)

**B. Google Sheets Integration (Secondary Storage):**

**GoogleSheetsService** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sheets_service.py`) maintains two worksheets:

1. **Sales Worksheet** - Columns:
   - Date, Product ID, Title, Platform, Sale Price, Original Cost
   - Shipping Cost, Platform Fee, Payment Fee, Net Profit
   - Buyer Info, Category, Brand, Size, Notes

2. **Inventory Worksheet** - Columns:
   - Product ID, Title, Price, Original Cost, Category, Brand
   - Size, Condition, Status, Marktplaats, Vinted, Depop, Facebook Marketplace
   - Created Date, Updated Date

**Sync Tracking Endpoint** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sync.py`):
- `/sync/stats` provides:
  - Total listings count
  - Listings needing sync
  - Listings with sync errors
  - Platform breakdown

---

## DATABASE CONFIGURATION

**Location:** `/home/filip/CRM/app/core/database.py`

```python
DATABASE_URL: str = "sqlite:///./crm.db"  # Default: SQLite
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)
Base = declarative_base()
```

**Features:**
- Using SQLAlchemy ORM
- Default to SQLite (easily switchable to PostgreSQL, MySQL)
- Automatic session management via `get_db()` dependency
- `init_db()` function creates all tables

---

## 7. DATA CONSTRAINTS AND VALIDATION

**Unique Constraints:**
- `Product.sku` - Must be globally unique

**Required Fields:**
- Product: id, sku, title, price
- PlatformListing: id, product_id, platform
- Sale: id, product_id, platform, sale_price, sale_date

**Nullable Fields:**
- Product: description, category, size, condition, brand, color, investment_per_product
- PlatformListing: platform_listing_id, listing_url, last_synced_at, sync_error
- Sale: buyer_info

**Default Values:**
- Product.quantity: 1
- Product.status: ACTIVE
- Product.images: [] (empty list)
- PlatformListing.platform_status: ACTIVE
- PlatformListing.needs_sync: True
- Sale.shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee: 0.0
- Sale.synced_to_sheets: False

---

## 8. KEY ENUMS

**ProductStatus:**
- ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, INACTIVE

**Platform:**
- MARKTPLAATS, VINTED, DEPOP, FACEBOOK_MARKETPLACE

**PlatformStatus:**
- ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, DELETED, ERROR

---

## 9. SCHEMA DEFINITIONS

**Pydantic Schemas** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/schemas/product.py`):
- `ProductBase` - Base fields for creation/update
- `ProductCreate` - Creation schema
- `ProductUpdate` - Update schema (all fields optional)
- `Product` - Response schema (with ID, timestamps, status)
- `PlatformListingInfo` - Nested listing info
- `ProductWithListings` - Product + platform listings

**API Response Schema for Sales** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`):
- `SaleResponse` - Returns: id, product_id, platform, sale_price, sale_date, net_profit, synced_to_sheets

---

## SUMMARY TABLE

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Tables** | 3 (products, platform_listings, sales) |
| **SKU Generation** | Auto-generated, format: PREFIX-HASH-TIMESTAMP |
| **Relationships** | Product (1) → PlatformListings (Many), Product (1) → Sales (Many) |
| **Multi-platform** | Yes - single product across 4 platforms |
| **Sync Tracking** | Yes - needs_sync flag, last_synced_at, sync_error |
| **Financial Fields** | sale_price, shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee, net_profit |
| **Analytics** | Computed via API queries; mirrored to Google Sheets |
| **Investment Tracking** | investment_per_product field + formula parser |
| **Audit Trails** | created_at, updated_at on all tables |

This architecture supports a typical reseller/marketplace management workflow with full financial tracking, multi-platform synchronization, and analytics capabilities.

- PlatformListing: platform_listing_id, listing_url, last_synced_at, sync_error
- Sale: buyer_info

**Default Values:**
- Product.quantity: 1
- Product.status: ACTIVE
- Product.images: [] (empty list)
- PlatformListing.platform_status: ACTIVE
- PlatformListing.needs_sync: True
- Sale.shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee: 0.0
- Sale.synced_to_sheets: False

---

## 8. KEY ENUMS

**ProductStatus:**
- ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, INACTIVE

**Platform:**
- MARKTPLAATS, VINTED, DEPOP, FACEBOOK_MARKETPLACE

**PlatformStatus:**
- ACTIVE, SOLD, PENDING, DELETED, ERROR

---

## 9. SCHEMA DEFINITIONS

**Pydantic Schemas** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/schemas/product.py`):
- `ProductBase` - Base fields for creation/update
- `ProductCreate` - Creation schema
- `ProductUpdate` - Update schema (all fields optional)
- `Product` - Response schema (with ID, timestamps, status)
- `PlatformListingInfo` - Nested listing info
- `ProductWithListings` - Product + platform listings

**API Response Schema for Sales** (`/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`):
- `SaleResponse` - Returns: id, product_id, platform, sale_price, sale_date, net_profit, synced_to_sheets

---

## SUMMARY TABLE

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Tables** | 3 (products, platform_listings, sales) |
| **SKU Generation** | Auto-generated, format: PREFIX-HASH-TIMESTAMP |
| **Relationships** | Product (1) → PlatformListings (Many), Product (1) → Sales (Many) |
| **Multi-platform** | Yes - single product across 4 platforms |
| **Sync Tracking** | Yes - needs_sync flag, last_synced_at, sync_error |
| **Financial Fields** | sale_price, shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee, net_profit |
| **Analytics** | Computed via API queries; mirrored to Google Sheets |
| **Investment Tracking** | investment_per_product field + formula parser |
| **Audit Trails** | created_at, updated_at on all tables |

This architecture supports a typical reseller/marketplace management workflow with full financial tracking, multi-platform synchronization, and analytics capabilities.

input.prompt:
Design a comprehensive implementation plan for enhancing this multi-platform reselling CRM with the following requirements:

## Context from Exploration:

**Current Architecture:**
- SQLite database with 3 models: Product (with SKU), PlatformListing, Sale
- 4 platform integrations: Marktplaats (OAuth), Vinted/Depop/Facebook (browser automation)
- Google Sheets integration exists (sales & inventory worksheets)
- Frontend dashboard with Chart.js, tabs for Products/Sales/Sync/Settings
- API endpoints for products, sales, sync operations

**User Requirements:**

1. **Google Sheets Import**
   - Import existing inventory data (with SKUs) and match to products
   - Import historical sales data for analytics
   - Two-way sync: CRM ↔ Google Sheets

2. **Enhanced Revenue Dashboard**
   - Daily, monthly, quarterly, yearly views
   - Revenue trends over time (line/area charts)
   - Inventory turnover and sell-through rate metrics
   - Profit margins and ROI tracking
   - Cashflow tracking alongside revenue and profits
   - Dynamic and visually appealing design

3. **Excel-like Editable Table**
   - Edit sales data inline (prices, fees, dates)
   - Add VAT field for Vinted products specifically
   - Edit without having to (automatic calculation/defaults)
   - Real-time updates to backend

4. **Monthly Recurring Costs Feature**
   - Track recurring expenses (subscriptions, fees, etc.)
   - Factor into monthly profit/cashflow calculations
   - Editable cost table

5. **Additional Features**
   - Bulk operations (select multiple products, cross-post, update, delete)
   - Automated alerts/notifications (sales, sync failures, errors)
   - Price optimization suggestions based on time-to-sell data

6. **Platform-Specific Considerations**
   - Primary: Vinted, Marktplaats
   - Secondary: Facebook Marketplace, Depop (sometimes)

## Design Requirements:

1. Create detailed database schema changes (new models/fields)
2. Design API endpoints for new features
3. Design frontend components and user flows
4. Identify libraries/tools needed (e.g., editable tables, charts, notifications)
5. Consider data migration strategy for existing products/sales
6. Design Google Sheets import/export flow with SKU matching
7. Design recurring costs tracking mechanism
8. Design cashflow calculation logic
9. Design notification system architecture
10. Design price optimization algorithm approach

Provide a comprehensive, step-by-step implementation plan organized by feature area. Include:
- Database changes
- Backend API changes
- Frontend UI changes
- Third-party libraries to add
- Migration scripts needed
- Critical files to modify
- Implementation order/dependencies

Focus on practical, actionable steps that build on the existing architecture.

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed implementation plan for all the requested enhancements. # Comprehensive Implementation Plan: Multi-Platform Reselling CRM Enhancement Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've designed a comprehensive implementation plan that builds on your existing FastAPI backend with SQLAlchemy, React-less frontend (vanilla JS + Chart.js), and Google Sheets integration. ## ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW **Current Stack:** - Backend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + SQLite - Frontend: Vanilla JavaScript + Chart.js - Database: 3 models (Product, PlatformListing, Sale) - Integrations: Google Sheets (gspread), 4 platforms (Marktplaats OAuth, Vinted/Depop/Facebook browser automation) --- ## PHASE 1: DATABASE SCHEMA ENHANCEMENTS ### 1.1 New Models Required **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/recurring_cost.py`:** ```python class RecurringCost(Base): __tablename__ = "recurring_costs" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False) description = Column(Text, nullable=True) amount = Column(Float, nullable=False) frequency = Column(Enum('monthly', 'quarterly', 'yearly'), default='monthly') category = Column(String(100), nullable=True) # e.g., "subscription", "platform_fee", "shipping" is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True) start_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False) end_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True) created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now()) updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now()) ``` **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/notification.py`:** ```python class NotificationType(str, enum.Enum): SALE = "sale" SYNC_ERROR = "sync_error" PRICE_SUGGESTION = "price_suggestion" INVENTORY_ALERT = "inventory_alert" class Notification(Base): __tablename__ = "notifications" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) type = Column(Enum(NotificationType), nullable=False) title = Column(String(255), nullable=False) message = Column(Text, nullable=False) read = Column(Boolean, default=False) metadata = Column(JSON, nullable=True) # Additional data (product_id, sale_id, etc.) created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now()) ``` **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product_metrics.py`:** ```python class ProductMetrics(Base): __tablename__ = "product_metrics" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False, unique=True) days_listed = Column(Integer, default=0) view_count = Column(Integer, default=0) times_price_reduced = Column(Integer, default=0) original_listing_price = Column(Float, nullable=True) optimal_price_suggestion = Column(Float, nullable=True) last_price_check = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True) updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now()) product = relationship("Product", back_populates="metrics") ``` ### 1.2 Modify Existing Models **Update `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/sale.py`:** ```python # Add new fields: vat_amount = Column(Float, default=0.0) # Vinted-specific VAT original_cost = Column(Float, default=0.0) # Track product cost basis notes = Column(Text, nullable=True) # For manual notes ``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product.py`:** ```python # Add relationship: metrics = relationship("ProductMetrics", back_populates="product", uselist=False, cascade="all, delete-orphan") ``` ### 1.3 Migration Script **Create `/home/filip/CRM/alembic/versions/001_add_enhanced_features.py`:** - Add new tables: recurring_costs, notifications, product_metrics - Add new columns to Sale: vat_amount, original_cost, notes - Add relationship from Product to ProductMetrics - Backfill product_metrics for existing products with days_listed calculation --- ## PHASE 2: BACKEND API ENHANCEMENTS ### 2.1 Google Sheets Import/Export Service **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sheets_service.py`:** Add methods: ```python def import_inventory_with_sku_matching(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Import inventory from Google Sheets and match to existing products by SKU. Creates new products if SKU not found. """ def import_historical_sales(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Import historical sales data from Google Sheets. Matches products by SKU or title, creates Sale records. """ def two_way_sync_inventory(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Bidirectional sync: Update CRM from Sheets and Sheets from CRM. Uses last_updated timestamps to determine sync direction. """ def export_recurring_costs(self) -> bool: """ Export recurring costs to a dedicated worksheet. """ ``` ### 2.2 Recurring Costs API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/recurring_costs.py`:** ```python @router.get("/", response_model=List[RecurringCostResponse]) def get_recurring_costs(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100, is_active: bool = True, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/", response_model=RecurringCostResponse) def create_recurring_cost(cost: RecurringCostCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.put("/{cost_id}", response_model=RecurringCostResponse) def update_recurring_cost(cost_id: int, cost_update: RecurringCostUpdate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.delete("/{cost_id}") def delete_recurring_cost(cost_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/monthly-total") def get_monthly_costs_total(year: int, month: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.3 Enhanced Analytics API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/analytics.py`:** ```python @router.get("/revenue/daily") def get_daily_revenue(start_date: datetime, end_date: datetime, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: [{date, revenue, profit, sales_count, avg_sale_price}] @router.get("/revenue/monthly") def get_monthly_revenue(year: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/revenue/quarterly") def get_quarterly_revenue(year: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/revenue/yearly") def get_yearly_revenue(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/cashflow") def get_cashflow_analysis(start_date: datetime, end_date: datetime, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: {revenue, costs, recurring_costs, net_cashflow, by_month: [...]} @router.get("/inventory/metrics") def get_inventory_metrics(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: {turnover_rate, avg_days_to_sell, sell_through_rate, dead_stock_count} @router.get("/profit-margins") def get_profit_margins(period: str = "30d", db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: {gross_margin, net_margin, roi, by_platform: {...}} ``` ### 2.4 Sales Update API (for editable table) **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`:** ```python @router.put("/{sale_id}", response_model=SaleResponse) def update_sale(sale_id: int, sale_update: SaleUpdate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Allow updating: sale_price, sale_date, shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee, vat_amount, notes @router.patch("/{sale_id}/quick-update") def quick_update_sale_field(sale_id: int, field: str, value: Any, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # For real-time inline edits @router.post("/bulk-update") def bulk_update_sales(updates: List[SaleUpdate], db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.5 Notifications API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/notifications.py`:** ```python @router.get("/", response_model=List[NotificationResponse]) def get_notifications(unread_only: bool = False, limit: int = 50, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/{notification_id}/mark-read") def mark_notification_read(notification_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/mark-all-read") def mark_all_read(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.delete("/{notification_id}") def delete_notification(notification_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.6 Bulk Operations API **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py`:** ```python @router.post("/bulk-cross-post") async def bulk_cross_post(product_ids: List[int], platforms: List[Platform], background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/bulk-update") def bulk_update_products(updates: Dict[int, ProductUpdate], db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/bulk-delete") def bulk_delete_products(product_ids: List[int], db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/bulk-status-change") def bulk_change_status(product_ids: List[int], new_status: ProductStatus, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.7 Price Optimization Service **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/price_optimization_service.py`:** ```python class PriceOptimizationService: def calculate_optimal_price(self, product_id: int) -> Optional[float]: """ Algorithm: 1. Get days_listed from ProductMetrics 2. Get average time-to-sell for similar products (category, brand, price range) 3. If days_listed > avg_time_to_sell * 1.5: suggest 10% price reduction 4. If days_listed > avg_time_to_sell * 2: suggest 20% price reduction 5. Consider platform-specific pricing (Vinted vs Marktplaats buyer behavior) """ def analyze_pricing_trends(self, category: str, days: int = 90) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Analyze pricing trends for a category to inform pricing decisions. Returns: {avg_sale_price, median_days_to_sell, price_ranges: {...}} """ async def suggest_price_adjustments(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: """ Find products that have been listed too long and suggest price adjustments. Creates notifications for suggested price changes. """ ``` ### 2.8 Sheets Import/Export Endpoints **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sheets.py`:** ```python @router.post("/import/inventory") async def import_inventory_from_sheets(background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/import/sales") async def import_sales_from_sheets(background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/export/inventory") async def export_inventory_to_sheets(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/export/sales") async def export_sales_to_sheets(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/sync/two-way") async def two_way_sync_sheets(background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` --- ## PHASE 3: FRONTEND ENHANCEMENTS ### 3.1 Enhanced Revenue Dashboard **Update `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`:** Add new dashboard sections: ```html

Inventory Turnover

-

Sell-Through Rate

-

Avg Days to Sell

-

Net Cashflow (30d)

€-

Gross Margin

-

ROI

-

``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** Add functions: ```javascript async function loadRevenueChart(period) { // Fetch data from /api/analytics/revenue/{period} // Create Chart.js area chart showing revenue trends over time // Add dual-axis for revenue and profit } async function loadCashflowChart() { // Fetch from /api/analytics/cashflow // Create stacked bar chart: revenue (positive) vs costs + recurring costs (negative) // Show net cashflow as a line overlay } async function loadInventoryMetrics() { // Fetch from /api/analytics/inventory/metrics // Update KPI cards } async function loadProfitMargins() { // Fetch from /api/analytics/profit-margins // Update margin KPI cards } ``` ### 3.2 Excel-like Editable Sales Table **Library to Add:** Handsontable (https://handsontable.com/) - Open source Excel-like grid **Update `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html` Sales Tab:** ```html
``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** ```javascript let salesTableInstance; function initSalesTable(salesData) { const container = document.getElementById('sales-table-container'); const data = salesData.map(sale => ({ id: sale.id, date: new Date(sale.sale_date).toLocaleDateString(), product_id: sale.product_id, platform: sale.platform, sale_price: sale.sale_price, shipping_cost: sale.shipping_cost || 0, platform_fee: sale.platform_fee || 0, payment_fee: sale.payment_fee || 0, vat: sale.vat_amount || 0, // Vinted-specific net_profit: sale.net_profit || 0, notes: sale.notes || '' })); salesTableInstance = new Handsontable(container, { data: data, colHeaders: ['ID', 'Date', 'Product ID', 'Platform', 'Sale Price', 'Shipping', 'Platform Fee', 'Payment Fee', 'VAT', 'Net Profit', 'Notes'], columns: [ { data: 'id', readOnly: true }, { data: 'date', type: 'date', dateFormat: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }, { data: 'product_id', readOnly: true }, { data: 'platform', type: 'dropdown', source: ['vinted', 'marktplaats', 'depop', 'facebook_marketplace'] }, { data: 'sale_price', type: 'numeric', numericFormat: { pattern: '€0,0.00' } }, { data: 'shipping_cost', type: 'numeric', numericFormat: { pattern: '€0,0.00' } }, { data: 'platform_fee', type: 'numeric', numericFormat: { pattern: '€0,0.00' } }, { data: 'payment_fee', type: 'numeric', numericFormat: { pattern: '€0,0.00' } }, { data: 'vat', type: 'numeric', numericFormat: { pattern: '€0,0.00' } }, { data: 'net_profit', type: 'numeric', numericFormat: { pattern: '€0,0.00' }, readOnly: true }, { data: 'notes', type: 'text' } ], afterChange: (changes, source) => { if (source === 'edit') { // Recalculate net_profit on any change recalculateNetProfit(changes); } }, licenseKey: 'non-commercial-and-evaluation' }); } function recalculateNetProfit(changes) { // Auto-calculate net_profit = sale_price - shipping - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat // Update the cell in real-time } async function saveSalesChanges() { const allData = salesTableInstance.getData(); const updates = allData.map((row, idx) => ({ id: row[0], sale_date: row[1], sale_price: row[4], shipping_cost: row[5], platform_fee: row[6], payment_fee: row[7], vat_amount: row[8], notes: row[10] }));
