${n.message}

${new Date(n.created_at).toLocaleString()}
`).join(''); document.getElementById('notifications-list').innerHTML = listHtml; } // Poll for new notifications every 30 seconds setInterval(loadNotifications, 30000); ``` ### 3.5 Bulk Operations UI **Update Products tab in `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`:** ```html
``` **Add to `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** ```javascript let bulkSelectMode = false; let selectedProducts = new Set(); function toggleBulkSelectMode() { bulkSelectMode = !bulkSelectMode; // Show/hide checkboxes on product cards // Show/hide bulk actions toolbar } async function bulkCrossPost() { const platforms = prompt('Enter platforms (comma-separated): vinted, marktplaats, depop, facebook_marketplace'); const platformList = platforms.split(',').map(p => p.trim()); await fetch(`${API_BASE}/products/bulk-cross-post`, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ product_ids: Array.from(selectedProducts), platforms: platformList }) }); } ``` ### 3.6 Google Sheets Import UI **Add to Settings tab:** ```html

Google Sheets Import/Export

``` --- ## PHASE 4: BACKGROUND TASKS & SCHEDULED JOBS ### 4.1 Create Background Task Manager **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/background_tasks.py`:** ```python from apscheduler.schedulers.background import BackgroundScheduler from app.services.price_optimization_service import PriceOptimizationService from app.models.notification import Notification, NotificationType scheduler = BackgroundScheduler() def check_for_price_optimization(): """Run every 24 hours to check for stale inventory and suggest price adjustments""" db = SessionLocal() price_service = PriceOptimizationService(db) suggestions = await price_service.suggest_price_adjustments() # Create notifications for each suggestion for suggestion in suggestions: notification = Notification( type=NotificationType.PRICE_SUGGESTION, title=f"Price adjustment suggested for {suggestion['product_title']}", message=f"Reduce price from €{suggestion['current_price']} to €{suggestion['suggested_price']}", metadata={"product_id": suggestion['product_id']} ) db.add(notification) db.commit() def auto_sync_to_sheets(): """Run every hour to sync new sales to Google Sheets""" db = SessionLocal() sheets_service = GoogleSheetsService() # Sync unsynced sales def check_sold_items_scheduled(): """Run every 15 minutes to check for sold items on all platforms""" db = SessionLocal() sync_service = SyncService(db) await sync_service.check_for_sold_items() # Schedule jobs scheduler.add_job(check_for_price_optimization, 'interval', hours=24) scheduler.add_job(auto_sync_to_sheets, 'interval', hours=1) scheduler.add_job(check_sold_items_scheduled, 'interval', minutes=15) scheduler.start() ``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/main.py`:** ```python from app.services.background_tasks import scheduler @app.on_event("startup") async def startup_event(): # ... existing code print("Starting background scheduler...") ``` --- ## PHASE 5: DATA MIGRATION & UTILITIES ### 5.1 Database Migration Script **Create `/home/filip/CRM/migrations/add_enhanced_features.py`:** ```python """ Migration script to add: 1. recurring_costs table 2. notifications table 3. product_metrics table 4. New fields to Sale model (vat_amount, original_cost, notes) 5. Backfill product_metrics with days_listed calculation """ def upgrade(): # Create new tables # Alter Sale table # Backfill ProductMetrics for existing products def downgrade(): # Rollback changes ``` ### 5.2 Google Sheets Import Helper **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py`:** ```python def match_product_by_sku(sku: str, db: Session) -> Optional[Product]: """Find product by SKU""" def fuzzy_match_product_by_title(title: str, db: Session, threshold: float = 0.85) -> Optional[Product]: """Fuzzy match product by title using difflib""" def parse_sheets_row_to_product(row: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]: """Convert Google Sheets row to Product schema""" def parse_sheets_row_to_sale(row: Dict[str, Any], db: Session) -> Optional[Sale]: """Convert Google Sheets row to Sale schema, matching product by SKU""" ``` --- ## PHASE 6: THIRD-PARTY LIBRARIES TO ADD **Update `/home/filip/CRM/requirements.txt`:** ``` # Existing libraries remain... # Add for enhanced analytics pandas==2.1.4 numpy==1.26.3 # Add for fuzzy matching (SKU import) fuzzywuzzy==0.18.0 python-Levenshtein==0.23.0 # Frontend (add to HTML via CDN): # - Handsontable: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/handsontable@latest/dist/handsontable.full.min.js # - Chart.js plugins for better charts: # - chartjs-plugin-datalabels # - chartjs-plugin-annotation ``` --- ## IMPLEMENTATION ORDER & DEPENDENCIES ### WEEK 1: Foundation 1. Database schema changes (Phase 1) 2. Run migrations 3. Create new models and schemas ### WEEK 2: Backend APIs 4. Recurring costs API (Phase 2.2) 5. Enhanced analytics API (Phase 2.3) 6. Sales update API for editable table (Phase 2.4) 7. Notifications API (Phase 2.5) ### WEEK 3: Google Sheets Integration 8. Enhance Google Sheets service (Phase 2.1) 9. Create sheets import/export endpoints (Phase 2.8) 10. Test SKU matching logic ### WEEK 4: Frontend - Dashboard 11. Enhanced revenue dashboard (Phase 3.1) 12. Add Chart.js area/line charts for trends 13. Add KPI cards with calculations ### WEEK 5: Frontend - Editable Tables 14. Integrate Handsontable for sales (Phase 3.2) 15. Recurring costs management UI (Phase 3.3) 16. Real-time calculation logic ### WEEK 6: Additional Features 17. Bulk operations UI and API (Phase 2.6, 3.5) 18. Notifications bell and panel (Phase 3.4) 19. Google Sheets import UI (Phase 3.6) ### WEEK 7: Intelligence Features 20. Price optimization service (Phase 2.7) 21. Background tasks scheduler (Phase 4) 22. Automated alerts integration ### WEEK 8: Testing & Polish 23. End-to-end testing of all features 24. Performance optimization 25. UI/UX polish and responsive design 26. Documentation --- ## CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION NOTES ### Database Considerations - **Migration strategy:** Use Alembic for schema migrations to avoid data loss - **Backup before migration:** Create backup of crm.db before running migrations - **Indexes:** Add indexes on Sale.sale_date, Product.sku, RecurringCost.is_active for query performance ### Google Sheets Import Logic ```python # SKU Matching Algorithm: 1. Try exact SKU match first 2. If not found, try fuzzy title match (>85% similarity) 3. If match found: update existing product 4. If no match: create new product with imported data 5. Log all imports with match status for user review ``` ### Cashflow Calculation ```python def calculate_monthly_cashflow(year: int, month: int) -> Dict[str, float]: # Revenue from sales in month revenue = sum(sales.sale_price for sales in get_sales_for_month(year, month)) # Variable costs (shipping, fees) from sales variable_costs = sum(sale.shipping_cost + sale.platform_fee + sale.payment_fee + sale.vat_amount for sale in get_sales_for_month(year, month)) # Recurring costs for the month recurring_costs = get_monthly_recurring_costs_total(year, month) # Net cashflow net_cashflow = revenue - variable_costs - recurring_costs return { "revenue": revenue, "variable_costs": variable_costs, "recurring_costs": recurring_costs, "net_cashflow": net_cashflow, "profit_margin": (net_cashflow / revenue * 100) if revenue > 0 else 0 } ``` ### Inventory Metrics Calculations ```python # Turnover Rate = Cost of Goods Sold / Average Inventory Value turnover_rate = total_sold_value_period / avg_inventory_value # Sell-Through Rate = Units Sold / Units Available * 100 sell_through_rate = (units_sold / (units_sold + units_remaining)) * 100 # Average Days to Sell = Sum(all sold products' days_listed) / count(sold products) avg_days_to_sell = sum(product_metrics.days_listed for sold_products) / count(sold_products) ``` ### Price Optimization Algorithm ```python def calculate_optimal_price(product: Product) -> float: metrics = product.metrics # Get category benchmarks avg_days_to_sell = get_category_avg_days_to_sell(product.category) # Calculate price adjustment factor if metrics.days_listed > avg_days_to_sell * 2: factor = 0.80 # 20% reduction elif metrics.days_listed > avg_days_to_sell * 1.5: factor = 0.90 # 10% reduction else: factor = 1.0 # No change # Apply platform-specific adjustments if primary_platform == Platform.VINTED: factor *= 0.95 # Vinted buyers expect lower prices suggested_price = product.price * factor # Round to nearest €0.50 return round(suggested_price * 2) / 2 ``` ### Real-time Editable Table Auto-calculations - On any field change in Handsontable, trigger recalculation: - `net_profit = sale_price - shipping_cost - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat_amount - original_cost` - Update cell immediately (no save needed) - Debounce API save by 2 seconds (batch multiple edits) --- ### Critical Files for Implementation **Database Layer:** - `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/recurring_cost.py` - New model for recurring expenses - `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/notification.py` - New model for alerts/notifications - `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product_metrics.py` - New model for product analytics - `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/sale.py` - Modify to add vat_amount, original_cost, notes fields - `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product.py` - Add metrics relationship **Backend API:** - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/analytics.py` - New analytics endpoints (revenue trends, cashflow, margins) - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/recurring_costs.py` - CRUD for recurring costs - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/notifications.py` - Notifications management - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sheets.py` - Google Sheets import/export endpoints - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py` - Enhance with bulk update and inline edit endpoints - `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py` - Add bulk operations (cross-post, update, delete) **Services:** - `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sheets_service.py` - Enhance with import methods (SKU matching, two-way sync) - `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/price_optimization_service.py` - New service for price suggestions - `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/background_tasks.py` - Scheduled jobs (price checks, auto-sync) **Frontend:** - `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html` - Major updates (new charts, editable tables, notifications bell, recurring costs tab) - `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js` - Extensive additions (Chart.js configs, Handsontable init, notification handling, bulk operations) - `/home/filip/CRM/static/css/styles.css` - Style enhancements (KPI cards, notifications panel, modal improvements) **Utilities:** - `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py` - Helper functions for SKU matching and fuzzy search - `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/analytics_calculations.py` - Reusable calculation functions (cashflow, margins, turnover) **Migration:** - `/home/filip/CRM/alembic/versions/001_add_enhanced_features.py` - Database migration script

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design a comprehensive implementation plan for enhancing this multi-platform reselling CRM with the following requirements:

## Context from Exploration:

**Current Architecture:**
- SQLite database with 3 models: Product (with SKU), PlatformListing, Sale
- 4 platform integrations: Marktplaats (OAuth), Vinted/Depop/Facebook (browser automation)
- Google Sheets integration exists (sales & inventory worksheets)
- Frontend dashboard with Chart.js, tabs for Products/Sales/Sync/Settings
- API endpoints for products, sales, sync operations

**User Requirements:**

1. **Google Sheets Import**
   - Import existing inventory data (with SKUs) and match to products
   - Import historical sales data for analytics
   - Two-way sync: CRM ↔ Google Sheets

2. **Enhanced Revenue Dashboard**
   - Daily, monthly, quarterly, yearly views
   - Revenue trends over time (line/area charts)
   - Inventory turnover and sell-through rate metrics
   - Profit margins and ROI tracking
   - Cashflow tracking alongside revenue and profits
   - Dynamic and visually appealing design

3. **Excel-like Editable Table**
   - Edit sales data inline (prices, fees, dates)
   - Add VAT field for Vinted products specifically
   - Edit without having to (automatic calculation/defaults)
   - Real-time updates to backend

4. **Monthly Recurring Costs Feature**
   - Track recurring expenses (subscriptions, fees, etc.)
   - Factor into monthly profit/cashflow calculations
   - Editable cost table

5. **Additional Features**
   - Bulk operations (select multiple products, cross-post, update, delete)
   - Automated alerts/notifications (sales, sync failures, errors)
   - Price optimization suggestions based on time-to-sell data

6. **Platform-Specific Considerations**
   - Primary: Vinted, Marktplaats
   - Secondary: Facebook Marketplace, Depop (sometimes)

## Design Requirements:

1. Create detailed database schema changes (new models/fields)
2. Design API endpoints for new features
3. Design frontend components and user flows
4. Identify libraries/tools needed (e.g., editable tables, charts, notifications)
5. Consider data migration strategy for existing products/sales
6. Design Google Sheets import/export flow with SKU matching
7. Design recurring costs tracking mechanism
8. Design cashflow calculation logic
9. Design notification system architecture
10. Design price optimization algorithm approach

Provide a comprehensive, step-by-step implementation plan organized by feature area. Include:
- Database changes
- Backend API changes
- Frontend UI changes
- Third-party libraries to add
- Migration scripts needed
- Critical files to modify
- Implementation order/dependencies

Focus on practical, actionable steps that build on the existing architecture.

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed implementation plan for all the requested enhancements. # Comprehensive Implementation Plan: Multi-Platform Reselling CRM Enhancement Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've designed a comprehensive implementation plan that builds on your existing FastAPI backend with SQLAlchemy, React-less frontend (vanilla JS + Chart.js), and Google Sheets integration. ## ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW **Current Stack:** - Backend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + SQLite - Frontend: Vanilla JavaScript + Chart.js - Database: 3 models (Product, PlatformListing, Sale) - Integrations: Google Sheets (gspread), 4 platforms (Marktplaats OAuth, Vinted/Depop/Facebook browser automation) --- ## PHASE 1: DATABASE SCHEMA ENHANCEMENTS ### 1.1 New Models Required **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/recurring_cost.py`:** ```python class RecurringCost(Base): __tablename__ = "recurring_costs" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) name = Column(String(255), nullable=False) description = Column(Text, nullable=True) amount = Column(Float, nullable=False) frequency = Column(Enum('monthly', 'quarterly', 'yearly'), default='monthly') category = Column(String(100), nullable=True) # e.g., "subscription", "platform_fee", "shipping" is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True) start_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False) end_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True) created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now()) updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now()) ``` **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/notification.py`:** ```python class NotificationType(str, enum.Enum): SALE = "sale" SYNC_ERROR = "sync_error" PRICE_SUGGESTION = "price_suggestion" INVENTORY_ALERT = "inventory_alert" class Notification(Base): __tablename__ = "notifications" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) type = Column(Enum(NotificationType), nullable=False) title = Column(String(255), nullable=False) message = Column(Text, nullable=False) read = Column(Boolean, default=False) metadata = Column(JSON, nullable=True) # Additional data (product_id, sale_id, etc.) created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now()) ``` **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product_metrics.py`:** ```python class ProductMetrics(Base): __tablename__ = "product_metrics" id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True) product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False, unique=True) days_listed = Column(Integer, default=0) view_count = Column(Integer, default=0) times_price_reduced = Column(Integer, default=0) original_listing_price = Column(Float, nullable=True) optimal_price_suggestion = Column(Float, nullable=True) last_price_check = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True) updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now()) product = relationship("Product", back_populates="metrics") ``` ### 1.2 Modify Existing Models **Update `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/sale.py`:** ```python # Add new fields: vat_amount = Column(Float, default=0.0) # Vinted-specific VAT original_cost = Column(Float, default=0.0) # Track product cost basis notes = Column(Text, nullable=True) # For manual notes ``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product.py`:** ```python # Add relationship: metrics = relationship("ProductMetrics", back_populates="product", uselist=False, cascade="all, delete-orphan") ``` ### 1.3 Migration Script **Create `/home/filip/CRM/alembic/versions/001_add_enhanced_features.py`:** - Add new tables: recurring_costs, notifications, product_metrics - Add new columns to Sale: vat_amount, original_cost, notes - Add relationship from Product to ProductMetrics - Backfill product_metrics for existing products with days_listed calculation --- ## PHASE 2: BACKEND API ENHANCEMENTS ### 2.1 Google Sheets Import/Export Service **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sheets_service.py`:** Add methods: ```python def import_inventory_with_sku_matching(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Import inventory from Google Sheets and match to existing products by SKU. Creates new products if SKU not found. """ def import_historical_sales(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Import historical sales data from Google Sheets. Matches products by SKU or title, creates Sale records. """ def two_way_sync_inventory(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Bidirectional sync: Update CRM from Sheets and Sheets from CRM. Uses last_updated timestamps to determine sync direction. """ def export_recurring_costs(self) -> bool: """ Export recurring costs to a dedicated worksheet. """ ``` ### 2.2 Recurring Costs API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/recurring_costs.py`:** ```python @router.get("/", response_model=List[RecurringCostResponse]) def get_recurring_costs(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100, is_active: bool = True, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/", response_model=RecurringCostResponse) def create_recurring_cost(cost: RecurringCostCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.put("/{cost_id}", response_model=RecurringCostResponse) def update_recurring_cost(cost_id: int, cost_update: RecurringCostUpdate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.delete("/{cost_id}") def delete_recurring_cost(cost_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/monthly-total") def get_monthly_costs_total(year: int, month: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.3 Enhanced Analytics API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/analytics.py`:** ```python @router.get("/revenue/daily") def get_daily_revenue(start_date: datetime, end_date: datetime, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: [{date, revenue, profit, sales_count, avg_sale_price}] @router.get("/revenue/monthly") def get_monthly_revenue(year: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/revenue/quarterly") def get_quarterly_revenue(year: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/revenue/yearly") def get_yearly_revenue(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.get("/cashflow") def get_cashflow_analysis(start_date: datetime, end_date: datetime, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: {revenue, costs, recurring_costs, net_cashflow, by_month: [...]} @router.get("/inventory/metrics") def get_inventory_metrics(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: {turnover_rate, avg_days_to_sell, sell_through_rate, dead_stock_count} @router.get("/profit-margins") def get_profit_margins(period: str = "30d", db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Returns: {gross_margin, net_margin, roi, by_platform: {...}} ``` ### 2.4 Sales Update API (for editable table) **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`:** ```python @router.put("/{sale_id}", response_model=SaleResponse) def update_sale(sale_id: int, sale_update: SaleUpdate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # Allow updating: sale_price, sale_date, shipping_cost, platform_fee, payment_fee, vat_amount, notes @router.patch("/{sale_id}/quick-update") def quick_update_sale_field(sale_id: int, field: str, value: Any, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) # For real-time inline edits @router.post("/bulk-update") def bulk_update_sales(updates: List[SaleUpdate], db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.5 Notifications API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/notifications.py`:** ```python @router.get("/", response_model=List[NotificationResponse]) def get_notifications(unread_only: bool = False, limit: int = 50, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/{notification_id}/mark-read") def mark_notification_read(notification_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/mark-all-read") def mark_all_read(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.delete("/{notification_id}") def delete_notification(notification_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.6 Bulk Operations API **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py`:** ```python @router.post("/bulk-cross-post") async def bulk_cross_post(product_ids: List[int], platforms: List[Platform], background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/bulk-update") def bulk_update_products(updates: Dict[int, ProductUpdate], db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/bulk-delete") def bulk_delete_products(product_ids: List[int], db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/bulk-status-change") def bulk_change_status(product_ids: List[int], new_status: ProductStatus, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` ### 2.7 Price Optimization Service **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/price_optimization_service.py`:** ```python class PriceOptimizationService: def calculate_optimal_price(self, product_id: int) -> Optional[float]: """ Algorithm: 1. Get days_listed from ProductMetrics 2. Get average time-to-sell for similar products (category, brand, price range) 3. If days_listed > avg_time_to_sell * 1.5: suggest 10% price reduction 4. If days_listed > avg_time_to_sell * 2: suggest 20% price reduction 5. Consider platform-specific pricing (Vinted vs Marktplaats buyer behavior) """ def analyze_pricing_trends(self, category: str, days: int = 90) -> Dict[str, Any]: """ Analyze pricing trends for a category to inform pricing decisions. Returns: {avg_sale_price, median_days_to_sell, price_ranges: {...}} """ async def suggest_price_adjustments(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: """ Find products that have been listed too long and suggest price adjustments. Creates notifications for suggested price changes. """ ``` ### 2.8 Sheets Import/Export Endpoints **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sheets.py`:** ```python @router.post("/import/inventory") async def import_inventory_from_sheets(background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/import/sales") async def import_sales_from_sheets(background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/export/inventory") async def export_inventory_to_sheets(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/export/sales") async def export_sales_to_sheets(db: Session = Depends(get_db)) @router.post("/sync/two-way") async def two_way_sync_sheets(background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) ``` --- ## PHASE 3: FRONTEND ENHANCEMENTS ### 3.1 Enhanced Revenue Dashboard **Update `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`:** Add new dashboard sections: ```html

Inventory Turnover

-

Sell-Through Rate

-

Avg Days to Sell

-

Net Cashflow (30d)

€-

Gross Margin

-

ROI

-

``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** Add functions: ```javascript async function loadRevenueChart(period) { // Fetch data from /api/analytics/revenue/{period} // Create Chart.js area chart showing revenue trends over time // Add dual-axis for revenue and profit } async function loadCashflowChart() { // Fetch from /api/analytics/cashflow // Create stacked bar chart: revenue (positive) vs costs + recurring costs (negative) // Show net cashflow as a line overlay } async function loadInventoryMetrics() { // Fetch from /api/analytics/inventory/metrics // Update KPI cards } async function loadProfitMargins() { // Fetch from /api/analytics/profit-margins // Update margin KPI cards } ``` ### 3.2 Excel-like Editable Sales Table **Library to Add:** Handsontable (https://handsontable.com/) - Open source Excel-like grid **Update `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html` Sales Tab:** ```html
``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** ```javascript let salesTableInstance; function initSalesTable(salesData) { const container = document.getElementById('sales-table-container'); const data = salesData.map(sale => ({ id: sale.id, date: new Date(sale.sale_date).toLocaleDateString(), product_id: sale.product_id, platform: sale.platform, sale_price: sale.sale_price, shipping_cost: sale.shipping_cost || 0, platform_fee: sale.platform_fee || 0, payment_fee: sale.payment_fee || 0, vat: sale.vat_amount || 0, // Vinted-specific net_profit: sale.net_profit || 0, notes: sale.notes || '' })); } }, licenseKey: 'non-commercial-and-evaluation' }); } function recalculateNetProfit(changes) { // Auto-calculate net_profit = sale_price - shipping - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat // Update the cell in real-time } async function saveSalesChanges() { const allData = salesTableInstance.getData(); End-to-end testing, bug fixes, UI polish --- ## Critical Files to Modify ### New Files to Create: - `app/models/recurring_cost.py` - `app/models/notification.py` - `app/models/product_metrics.py` - `app/api/recurring_costs.py` - `app/api/analytics.py` - `app/api/notifications.py` - `app/api/sheets.py` - `app/services/price_optimization_service.py` - `app/services/background_tasks.py` - `app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py` - `app/utils/analytics_calculations.py` - `app/schemas/recurring_cost.py` - `app/schemas/notification.py` - `migrate_enhanced_features.py` ### Files to Modify: - `app/models/sale.py` - Add 3 new fields - `app/models/product.py` - Add metrics relationship - `app/services/sheets_service.py` - Add import/sync methods - `app/api/sales.py` - Add bulk update endpoints - `app/api/products.py` - Add bulk operations endpoints - `templates/dashboard.html` - Major UI additions - `static/js/app.js` - Extensive JavaScript additions - `static/css/styles.css` - Style enhancements - `main.py` - Register new routers, start scheduler - `requirements.txt` - Add new dependencies --- ## Key Technical Decisions ### Why Handsontable over custom solution? - Excel-like UX out of the box - Built-in validation, formatting, cell types - Auto-calculation support with `afterChange` hooks - Free for non-commercial use ### Why APScheduler over Celery? - Simpler setup (no Redis/RabbitMQ required) - Sufficient for periodic tasks (not high-volume queue) - Runs in-process with FastAPI ### Why fuzzy matching for SKU import? - Handles typos and variations in manual entry - Falls back to title matching if SKU missing in sheets - Minimizes manual reconciliation work ### Cashflow Calculation Design: ``` Net Cashflow = Revenue - Variable Costs - Recurring Costs Revenue = Sum of all sale_price Variable Costs = Sum of (shipping_cost + platform_fee + payment_fee + vat_amount) Recurring Costs = Monthly prorated costs from RecurringCost table ``` ### Database Performance: - Add indexes on: Sale.sale_date, Product.sku, RecurringCost.is_active - Use date range filters in queries to limit result sets - Cache analytics calculations (30 min TTL) for dashboard --- ## Risk Mitigation ### Data Migration: - Backup crm.db before running migration - Test migration on copy first - Migration script includes rollback capability ### Google Sheets Import: - Dry-run mode to preview matches before import - Log all SKU mismatches for manual review - Validate data types before insertion ### Performance: - Paginate large tables (sales, products) - Implement query result caching for analytics - Use background tasks for slow operations (import, bulk updates) ### User Experience: - Progress indicators for all async operations - Error messages with actionable guidance - Undo capability for bulk operations - Confirmation dialogs for destructive actions --- ## Success Criteria ✅ Import 100+ products from Google Sheets with 95%+ SKU match rate ✅ Dashboard loads revenue charts in <2 seconds ✅ Editable sales table supports real-time calculation ✅ Bulk cross-post 20+ products without errors ✅ Price optimization suggests adjustments for stale inventory ✅ Notifications appear within 30 seconds of events ✅ Cashflow calculations match manual Excel verification ✅ Two-way sync maintains data consistency between CRM and Sheets

# Multi-Platform CRM Enhancement Plan ## Overview Comprehensive enhancement of the reselling CRM with Google Sheets import, advanced revenue analytics, editable tables, recurring costs tracking, and intelligent features. ## User Requirements Summary - Import existing inventory and sales data from Google Sheets (SKU matching) - Enhanced dashboard with daily/monthly/quarterly/yearly revenue views - Inventory turnover, sell-through rate, profit margins, ROI, and cashflow tracking - Excel-like editable table for sales data with VAT field (Vinted-specific) - Monthly recurring costs tracking - Bulk operations (cross-post, update, delete) - Automated notifications for sales, sync errors, price suggestions - Price optimization based on time-to-sell analytics --- ## Phase 1: Database Schema Changes ### 1.1 New Models to Create **`/home/filip/CRM/app/models/recurring_cost.py`** ```python class RecurringCost(Base): __tablename__ = "recurring_costs" id, name, description, amount, frequency (monthly/quarterly/yearly) category, is_active, start_date, end_date created_at, updated_at ``` **`/home/filip/CRM/app/models/notification.py`** ```python class Notification(Base): __tablename__ = "notifications" id, type (sale/sync_error/price_suggestion/inventory_alert) title, message, read, metadata (JSON), created_at ``` **`/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product_metrics.py`** ```python class ProductMetrics(Base): __tablename__ = "product_metrics" id, product_id (FK), days_listed, view_count, times_price_reduced original_listing_price, optimal_price_suggestion, last_price_check, updated_at ``` ### 1.2 Modify Existing Models **`/home/filip/CRM/app/models/sale.py`** - Add fields: - `vat_amount` (Float) - Vinted VAT tracking - `original_cost` (Float) - Product cost basis - `notes` (Text) - Manual notes **`/home/filip/CRM/app/models/product.py`** - Add relationship: - `metrics` relationship to ProductMetrics ### 1.3 Create Migration Script **`/home/filip/CRM/migrate_enhanced_features.py`** - Create 3 new tables - Add 3 new columns to Sale - Backfill ProductMetrics with days_listed calculation for existing products --- ## Phase 2: Backend API Development ### 2.1 Recurring Costs API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/recurring_costs.py`** Endpoints: - `GET /api/recurring-costs/` - List all costs (filter by active) - `POST /api/recurring-costs/` - Create new recurring cost - `PUT /api/recurring-costs/{id}` - Update cost - `DELETE /api/recurring-costs/{id}` - Delete cost - `GET /api/recurring-costs/monthly-total` - Calculate total for specific month/year ### 2.2 Analytics API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/analytics.py`** Endpoints: - `GET /api/analytics/revenue/daily` - Daily revenue breakdown - `GET /api/analytics/revenue/monthly` - Monthly revenue (year parameter) - `GET /api/analytics/revenue/quarterly` - Quarterly revenue - `GET /api/analytics/revenue/yearly` - Yearly revenue trends - `GET /api/analytics/cashflow` - Revenue vs costs vs cashflow analysis - `GET /api/analytics/inventory/metrics` - Turnover rate, sell-through, avg days to sell - `GET /api/analytics/profit-margins` - Gross margin, net margin, ROI by platform **Key Calculations:** - Cashflow = Revenue - Variable Costs - Recurring Costs - Turnover Rate = COGS / Avg Inventory Value - Sell-Through Rate = Units Sold / Total Units * 100 - Avg Days to Sell = Sum(sold products days_listed) / Count ### 2.3 Sales Update API (Editable Table Support) **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sales.py`** Add endpoints: - `PUT /api/sales/{id}` - Full sale update - `PATCH /api/sales/{id}/quick-update` - Single field update (real-time) - `POST /api/sales/bulk-update` - Batch update multiple sales ### 2.4 Notifications API **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/notifications.py`** Endpoints: - `GET /api/notifications/` - List notifications (filter: unread_only) - `POST /api/notifications/{id}/mark-read` - Mark single as read - `POST /api/notifications/mark-all-read` - Mark all as read - `DELETE /api/notifications/{id}` - Delete notification ### 2.5 Bulk Operations API **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py`** Add endpoints: - `POST /api/products/bulk-cross-post` - Cross-post multiple products - `POST /api/products/bulk-update` - Update multiple products - `POST /api/products/bulk-delete` - Delete multiple products - `POST /api/products/bulk-status-change` - Change status for multiple ### 2.6 Google Sheets Integration **Enhance `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/sheets_service.py`** Add methods: - `import_inventory_with_sku_matching()` - Import from Sheets, match by SKU - `import_historical_sales()` - Import sales data, match products by SKU - `two_way_sync_inventory()` - Bidirectional sync using timestamps - `export_recurring_costs()` - Export recurring costs to worksheet **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/sheets.py`** Endpoints: - `POST /api/sheets/import/inventory` - Trigger inventory import - `POST /api/sheets/import/sales` - Trigger sales import - `POST /api/sheets/export/inventory` - Export inventory - `POST /api/sheets/export/sales` - Export sales - `POST /api/sheets/sync/two-way` - Two-way sync **SKU Matching Algorithm:** 1. Try exact SKU match 2. If not found, try fuzzy title match (>85% similarity using fuzzywuzzy) 3. If match found: update existing product 4. If no match: create new product 5. Log all imports with match status ### 2.7 Price Optimization Service **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/price_optimization_service.py`** Methods: - `calculate_optimal_price(product_id)` - Suggest price based on days_listed vs category average - `analyze_pricing_trends(category, days)` - Category pricing insights - `suggest_price_adjustments()` - Find stale inventory, create notifications **Algorithm:** - If days_listed > category_avg * 2: suggest 20% reduction - If days_listed > category_avg * 1.5: suggest 10% reduction - Apply platform-specific factors (Vinted: -5%) --- ## Phase 3: Frontend Enhancements ### 3.1 Enhanced Revenue Dashboard **Update `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`** Add sections: - Time period selector (Daily/Monthly/Quarterly/Yearly buttons) - Revenue trend chart (Chart.js area chart with dual-axis for revenue + profit) - Cashflow chart (stacked bar: revenue positive, costs negative, net line overlay) - 6 KPI cards: Inventory Turnover, Sell-Through Rate, Avg Days to Sell, Net Cashflow, Gross Margin, ROI **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`** Add functions: - `loadRevenueChart(period)` - Fetch and render revenue trends - `loadCashflowChart()` - Render cashflow analysis - `loadInventoryMetrics()` - Update KPI cards - `loadProfitMargins()` - Update margin KPIs **Chart.js Configuration:** - Use Chart.js area charts for trends (gradient fills) - Dual Y-axis for revenue (left) and profit (right) - Interactive tooltips with detailed breakdowns - Responsive and mobile-friendly ### 3.2 Excel-like Editable Sales Table **Library:** Handsontable (open-source Excel-like grid) **Update Sales Tab in `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`:** ```html
``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** ```javascript function initSalesTable(salesData) { salesTableInstance = new Handsontable(container, { columns: [ { data: 'id', readOnly: true }, { data: 'date', type: 'date' }, { data: 'sale_price', type: 'numeric', format: '€0,0.00' }, { data: 'shipping_cost', type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'platform_fee', type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'payment_fee', type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'vat', type: 'numeric' }, // Vinted-specific { data: 'net_profit', readOnly: true, type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'notes', type: 'text' } ], afterChange: recalculateNetProfit // Auto-calculate on edit }); } function recalculateNetProfit(changes) { // net_profit = sale_price - shipping - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat - original_cost // Update cell in real-time } ``` Features: - Inline editing with auto-calculation - Date picker for sale_date - Number formatting (€ currency) - Debounced auto-save (2 seconds after last edit) - VAT column visible/highlighted for Vinted sales ### 3.3 Recurring Costs Management **Add new tab:** ```html ``` **Tab content:** - Editable table (Handsontable) for costs - "Add Recurring Cost" button → modal form - Monthly costs summary card - Fields: Name, Amount, Frequency (dropdown), Category, Start Date, End Date ### 3.4 Notifications System **Update header in `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`:** ```html

Notifications

``` **Features:** - Real-time notification count badge - Slide-out panel on bell click - Notification types: sale, sync_error, price_suggestion, inventory_alert - Click notification to mark as read and navigate to relevant item - Auto-refresh every 30 seconds ### 3.5 Bulk Operations UI **Update Products tab:** ```html
``` **Product cards in bulk mode:** - Show checkbox on each card - Track selected products in Set - Enable bulk actions toolbar when >0 selected ### 3.6 Google Sheets Import UI **Add to Settings tab:** ```html

Google Sheets Import/Export

``` **Import flow:** 1. Click import button 2. Show progress indicator 3. Call API endpoint 4. Display results: X matched, Y created, Z errors 5. Show log of any SKU mismatches --- ## Phase 4: Background Tasks & Automation ### 4.1 Scheduled Jobs **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/background_tasks.py`** Using APScheduler: - `check_for_price_optimization()` - Every 24 hours: analyze stale inventory, create price suggestion notifications - `auto_sync_to_sheets()` - Every 1 hour: sync new sales to Google Sheets - `check_sold_items_scheduled()` - Every 15 minutes: poll platforms for sold items - `update_product_metrics()` - Daily: update days_listed for all active products **Update `/home/filip/CRM/main.py`:** ```python from app.services.background_tasks import scheduler @app.on_event("startup") async def startup_event(): print("Starting background scheduler...") # scheduler already started in background_tasks.py ``` --- ## Phase 5: Utilities & Helpers ### 5.1 Google Sheets Import Helper **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py`** Functions: - `match_product_by_sku(sku, db)` - Exact SKU match - `fuzzy_match_product_by_title(title, db, threshold=0.85)` - Fuzzy title matching - `parse_sheets_row_to_product(row)` - Convert Sheets row to Product dict - `parse_sheets_row_to_sale(row, db)` - Convert Sheets row to Sale dict ### 5.2 Analytics Calculations **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/analytics_calculations.py`** Functions: - `calculate_monthly_cashflow(year, month, db)` - Full cashflow breakdown - `calculate_inventory_turnover(db)` - Turnover rate - `calculate_sell_through_rate(db)` - Sell-through percentage - `calculate_profit_margins(sales, recurring_costs)` - Margin calculations --- ## Dependencies & Libraries ### Backend (add to requirements.txt): ``` pandas==2.1.4 numpy==1.26.3 fuzzywuzzy==0.18.0 python-Levenshtein==0.23.0 apscheduler==3.10.4 ``` ### Frontend (add to dashboard.html via CDN): ```html ``` --- ## Implementation Order (8 Weeks) ### Week 1: Database Foundation 1. ``` **Update `/home/filip/CRM/static/js/app.js`:** ```javascript function initSalesTable(salesData) { salesTableInstance = new Handsontable(container, { columns: [ { data: 'id', readOnly: true }, { data: 'date', type: 'date' }, { data: 'sale_price', type: 'numeric', format: '€0,0.00' }, { data: 'shipping_cost', type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'platform_fee', type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'payment_fee', type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'vat', type: 'numeric' }, // Vinted-specific { data: 'net_profit', readOnly: true, type: 'numeric' }, { data: 'notes', type: 'text' } ], afterChange: recalculateNetProfit // Auto-calculate on edit }); } function recalculateNetProfit(changes) { // net_profit = sale_price - shipping - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat - original_cost // Update cell in real-time } ``` Features: - Inline editing with auto-calculation - Date picker for sale_date - Number formatting (€ currency) - Debounced auto-save (2 seconds after last edit) - VAT column visible/highlighted for Vinted sales ### 3.3 Recurring Costs Management **Add new tab:** ```html ``` **Tab content:** - Editable table (Handsontable) for costs - "Add Recurring Cost" button → modal form - Monthly costs summary card - Fields: Name, Amount, Frequency (dropdown), Category, Start Date, End Date ### 3.4 Notifications System **Update header in `/home/filip/CRM/templates/dashboard.html`:** ```html

``` **Product cards in bulk mode:** - Show checkbox on each card - Track selected products in Set - Enable bulk actions toolbar when >0 selected ### 3.6 Google Sheets Import UI **Add to Settings tab:** ```html

Show log of any SKU mismatches --- ## Phase 4: Background Tasks & Automation ### 4.1 Scheduled Jobs **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/services/background_tasks.py`** Using APScheduler: - `check_for_price_optimization()` - Every 24 hours: analyze stale inventory, create price suggestion notifications - `auto_sync_to_sheets()` - Every 1 hour: sync new sales to Google Sheets - `check_sold_items_scheduled()` - Every 15 minutes: poll platforms for sold items - `update_product_metrics()` - Daily: update days_listed for all active products **Update `/home/filip/CRM/main.py`:** ```python from app.services.background_tasks import scheduler @app.on_event("startup") async def startup_event(): print("Starting background scheduler...") # scheduler already started in background_tasks.py ``` --- ## Phase 5: Utilities & Helpers ### 5.1 Google Sheets Import Helper **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py`** Functions: - `match_product_by_sku(sku, db)` - Exact SKU match - `fuzzy_match_product_by_title(title, db, threshold=0.85)` - Fuzzy title matching - `parse_sheets_row_to_product(row)` - Convert Sheets row to Product dict - `parse_sheets_row_to_sale(row, db)` - Convert Sheets row to Sale dict ### 5.2 Analytics Calculations **Create `/home/filip/CRM/app/utils/analytics_calculations.py`** Functions: - `calculate_monthly_cashflow(year, month, db)` - Full cashflow breakdown - `calculate_inventory_turnover(db)` - Turnover rate - `calculate_sell_through_rate(db)` - Sell-through percentage - `calculate_profit_margins(sales, recurring_costs)` - Margin calculations --- ## Dependencies & Libraries ### Backend (add to requirements.txt): ``` pandas==2.1.4 numpy==1.26.3 fuzzywuzzy==0.18.0 python-Levenshtein==0.23.0 apscheduler==3.10.4 ``` ### Frontend (add to dashboard.html via CDN): ```html ``` --- ## Implementation Order (8 Weeks) ### Week 1: Database Foundation 1. Implement cashflow chart 19. Add 6 KPI cards with real-time data 20. Style enhancements for visual appeal ### Week 6: Editable Tables & Recurring Costs 21. Integrate Handsontable for sales table 22. Implement auto-calculation for net_profit 23. Add recurring costs tab and table 24. Test inline editing and auto-save ### Week 7: Bulk Operations & Notifications 25. Implement bulk operations API and UI 26. Add notifications bell and panel 27. Integrate notification polling 28. Add Google Sheets import UI in Settings ### Week 8: Intelligence & Polish 29. Implement Price Optimization Service 30. Set up background scheduler with jobs 31. Create automated price suggestion notifications 32. End-to-end testing, bug fixes, UI polish --- ## Critical Files to Modify ### New Files to Create: - `app/models/recurring_cost.py` - `app/models/notification.py` - `app/models/product_metrics.py` - `app/api/recurring_costs.py` - `app/api/analytics.py` - `app/api/notifications.py` - `app/api/sheets.py` - `app/services/price_optimization_service.py` - `app/services/background_tasks.py` - `app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py` - `app/utils/analytics_calculations.py` - `app/schemas/recurring_cost.py` - `app/schemas/notification.py` - `migrate_enhanced_features.py` ### Files to Modify: - `app/models/sale.py` - Add 3 new fields - `app/models/product.py` - Add metrics relationship - `app/services/sheets_service.py` - Add import/sync methods - `app/api/sales.py` - Add bulk update endpoints - `app/api/products.py` - Add bulk operations endpoints - `templates/dashboard.html` - Major UI additions - `static/js/app.js` - Extensive JavaScript additions - `static/css/styles.css` - Style enhancements - `main.py` - Register new routers, start scheduler - `requirements.txt` - Add new dependencies --- ## Key Technical Decisions ### Why Handsontable over custom solution? - Excel-like UX out of the box - Built-in validation, formatting, cell types - Auto-calculation support with `afterChange` hooks - Free for non-commercial use ### Why APScheduler over Celery? - Simpler setup (no Redis/RabbitMQ required) - Sufficient for periodic tasks (not high-volume queue) - Runs in-process with FastAPI ### Why fuzzy matching for SKU import? - Handles typos and variations in manual entry - Falls back to title matching if SKU missing in sheets - Minimizes manual reconciliation work ### Cashflow Calculation Design: ``` Net Cashflow = Revenue - Variable Costs - Recurring Costs Revenue = Sum of all sale_price Variable Costs = Sum of (shipping_cost + platform_fee + payment_fee + vat_amount) Recurring Costs = Monthly prorated costs from RecurringCost table ``` ### Database Performance: - Add indexes on: Sale.sale_date, Product.sku, RecurringCost.is_active - Use date range filters in queries to limit result sets - Cache analytics calculations (30 min TTL) for dashboard --- ## Risk Mitigation ### Data Migration: - Backup crm.db before running migration - Test migration on copy first - Migration script includes rollback capability ### Google Sheets Import: - Dry-run mode to preview matches before import - Log all SKU mismatches for manual review - Validate data types before insertion ### Performance: - Paginate large tables (sales, products) - Implement query result caching for analytics - Use background tasks for slow operations (import, bulk updates) ### User Experience: - Progress indicators for all async operations - Error messages with actionable guidance - Undo capability for bulk operations - Confirmation dialogs for destructive actions --- ## Success Criteria ✅ Import 100+ products from Google Sheets with 95%+ SKU match rate ✅ Dashboard loads revenue charts in <2 seconds ✅ Editable sales table supports real-time calculation ✅ Bulk cross-post 20+ products without errors ✅ Price optimization suggests adjustments for stale inventory ✅ Notifications appear within 30 seconds of events ✅ Cashflow calculations match manual Excel verification ✅ Two-way sync maintains data consistency between CRM and Sheets

Create new models (RecurringCost, Notification, ProductMetrics) 2. Modify Sale model (add vat_amount, original_cost, notes) 3. Create migration script 4. Run migration, test database integrity ### Week 2: Core Backend APIs 5. Implement Recurring Costs API 6. Implement Notifications API 7. Enhance Sales API with bulk update endpoints 8. Test all CRUD operations ### Week 3: Analytics Backend 9. Create Analytics API with all endpoints 10. Implement analytics calculation utilities 11. Test revenue/cashflow/margin calculations 12. Ensure performance with indexes ### Week 4: Google Sheets Integration 13. Enhance GoogleSheetsService with import methods 14. Create SKU matching and fuzzy search helpers 15. Implement Sheets API endpoints 16. Test import with sample data (inventory + sales) ### Week 5: Frontend Dashboard 17. Add time period selector and revenue trend charts 18. Implement cashflow chart 19. Add 6 KPI cards with real-time data 20. Style enhancements for visual appeal ### Week 6: Editable Tables & Recurring Costs 21. Integrate Handsontable for sales table 22. Implement auto-calculation for net_profit 23. Add recurring costs tab and table 24. Test inline editing and auto-save ### Week 7: Bulk Operations & Notifications 25. Implement bulk operations API and UI 26. Add notifications bell and panel 27. Integrate notification polling 28. Add Google Sheets import UI in Settings ### Week 8: Intelligence & Polish 29. Implement Price Optimization Service 30. Set up background scheduler with jobs 31. Create automated price suggestion notifications 32. End-to-end testing, bug fixes, UI polish --- ## Critical Files to Modify ### New Files to Create: - `app/models/recurring_cost.py` - `app/models/notification.py` - `app/models/product_metrics.py` - `app/api/recurring_costs.py` - `app/api/analytics.py` - `app/api/notifications.py` - `app/api/sheets.py` - `app/services/price_optimization_service.py` - `app/services/background_tasks.py` - `app/utils/sheets_import_helper.py` - `app/utils/analytics_calculations.py` - `app/schemas/recurring_cost.py` - `app/schemas/notification.py` - `migrate_enhanced_features.py` ### Files to Modify: - `app/models/sale.py` - Add 3 new fields - `app/models/product.py` - Add metrics relationship - `app/services/sheets_service.py` - Add import/sync methods - `app/api/sales.py` - Add bulk update endpoints - `app/api/products.py` - Add bulk operations endpoints - `templates/dashboard.html` - Major UI additions - `static/js/app.js` - Extensive JavaScript additions - `static/css/styles.css` - Style enhancements - `main.py` - Register new routers, start scheduler - `requirements.txt` - Add new dependencies --- ## Key Technical Decisions ### Why Handsontable over custom solution? - Excel-like UX out of the box - Built-in validation, formatting, cell types - Auto-calculation support with `afterChange` hooks - Free for non-commercial use ### Why APScheduler over Celery? - Simpler setup (no Redis/RabbitMQ required) - Sufficient for periodic tasks (not high-volume queue) - Runs in-process with FastAPI ### Why fuzzy matching for SKU import? - Handles typos and variations in manual entry - Falls back to title matching if SKU missing in sheets - Minimizes manual reconciliation work ### Cashflow Calculation Design: ``` Net Cashflow = Revenue - Variable Costs - Recurring Costs Revenue = Sum of all sale_price Variable Costs = Sum of (shipping_cost + platform_fee + payment_fee + vat_amount) Recurring Costs = Monthly prorated costs from RecurringCost table ``` ### Database Performance: - Add indexes on: Sale.sale_date, Product.sku, RecurringCost.is_active - Use date range filters in queries to limit result sets - Cache analytics calculations (30 min TTL) for dashboard --- ## Risk Mitigation ### Data Migration: - Backup crm.db before running migration - Test migration on copy first - Migration script includes rollback capability ### Google Sheets Import: - Dry-run mode to preview matches before import - Log all SKU mismatches for manual review - Validate data types before insertion ### Performance: - Paginate large tables (sales, products) - Implement query result caching for analytics - Use background tasks for slow operations (import, bulk updates) ### User Experience: - Progress indicators for all async operations - Error messages with actionable guidance - Undo capability for bulk operations - Confirmation dialogs for destructive actions --- ## Success Criteria ✅ Import 100+ products from Google Sheets with 95%+ SKU match rate ✅ Dashboard loads revenue charts in <2 seconds ✅ Editable sales table supports real-time calculation ✅ Bulk cross-post 20+ products without errors ✅ Price optimization suggests adjustments for stale inventory ✅ Notifications appear within 30 seconds of events ✅ Cashflow calculations match manual Excel verification ✅ Two-way sync maintains data consistency between CRM and Sheets

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, DateTime, Text, Boolean, Enum
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from app.core.database import Base
import enum


class CostFrequency(str, enum.Enum):
    MONTHLY = "monthly"
    QUARTERLY = "quarterly"
    YEARLY = "yearly"


class RecurringCost(Base):
    __tablename__ = "recurring_costs"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    frequency = Column(Enum(CostFrequency), default=CostFrequency.MONTHLY)
    category = Column(String(100), nullable=True)  # e.g., "subscription", "platform_fee", "shipping"
    is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True, index=True)
    start_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
    end_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<RecurringCost(id={self.id}, name='{self.name}', amount={self.amount}, frequency='{self.frequency}')>"

    def calculate_monthly_cost(self) -> float:
        """Calculate the monthly equivalent cost based on frequency"""
        if self.frequency == CostFrequency.MONTHLY:
            return self.amount
        elif self.frequency == CostFrequency.QUARTERLY:
            return self.amount / 3
        elif self.frequency == CostFrequency.YEARLY:
            return self.amount / 12
        return 0.0

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, Float, DateTime, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from app.core.database import Base


class ProductMetrics(Base):
    __tablename__ = "product_metrics"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False, unique=True, index=True)
    days_listed = Column(Integer, default=0)
    view_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    times_price_reduced = Column(Integer, default=0)
    original_listing_price = Column(Float, nullable=True)
    optimal_price_suggestion = Column(Float, nullable=True)
    last_price_check = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())

    # Relationships
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="metrics")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ProductMetrics(id={self.id}, product_id={self.product_id}, days_listed={self.days_listed})>"

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, DateTime, ForeignKey, Boolean, JSON, Enum
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from app.core.database import Base
from app.models.platform_listing import Platform


class Sale(Base):
    __tablename__ = "sales"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("products.id"), nullable=False)
    platform = Column(Enum(Platform), nullable=False)

    # Sale details
    sale_price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    sale_date = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
    buyer_info = Column(JSON, nullable=True)  # Store buyer details if available

    # Tracking & fees
    shipping_cost = Column(Float, default=0.0)
    platform_fee = Column(Float, default=0.0)
    payment_fee = Column(Float, default=0.0)
    net_profit = Column(Float, nullable=True)  # sale_price - fees - original_cost

    # Google Sheets sync
    synced_to_sheets = Column(Boolean, default=False)
    sheets_row_number = Column(Integer, nullable=True)

    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())

    # Relationships
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="sales")

    def calculate_net_profit(self, original_cost: float = 0.0):
        """Calculate net profit after all fees"""
        self.net_profit = self.sale_price - self.shipping_cost - self.platform_fee - self.payment_fee - original_cost
        return self.net_profit

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Sale(id={self.id}, product_id={self.product_id}, platform='{self.platform}', price={self.sale_price})>"

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, DateTime, Text, JSON, Enum
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from app.core.database import Base
import enum


class ProductStatus(str, enum.Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    SOLD = "sold"
    PENDING = "pending"
    INACTIVE = "inactive"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = "products"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    sku = Column(String(100), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    title = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False, default=1)
    investment_per_product = Column(Float, nullable=True)
    images = Column(JSON, default=list)  # List of image URLs
    category = Column(String(100), nullable=True)
    size = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    condition = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    brand = Column(String(100), nullable=True)
    color = Column(String(50), nullable=True)
    status = Column(Enum(ProductStatus), default=ProductStatus.ACTIVE)

    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())

    # Relationships
    platform_listings = relationship("PlatformListing", back_populates="product", cascade="all, delete-orphan")
    sales = relationship("Sale", back_populates="product", cascade="all, delete-orphan")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(id={self.id}, title='{self.title}', status='{self.status}')>"

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional
from datetime import datetime
from app.models.recurring_cost import CostFrequency


class RecurringCostBase(BaseModel):
    name: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    amount: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    frequency: CostFrequency = CostFrequency.MONTHLY
    category: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=100)
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: Optional[datetime] = None


class RecurringCostCreate(RecurringCostBase):
    pass


class RecurringCostUpdate(BaseModel):
    name: Optional[str] = Field(None, min_length=1, max_length=255)
    description: Optional[str] = None
    amount: Optional[float] = Field(None, gt=0)
    frequency: Optional[CostFrequency] = None
    category: Optional[str] = Field(None, max_length=100)
    is_active: Optional[bool] = None
    start_date: Optional[datetime] = None
    end_date: Optional[datetime] = None


class RecurringCost(RecurringCostBase):
    id: int
    is_active: bool
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    class Config:
        from_attributes = True

input.content:
"""
Migration script to add enhanced CRM features:
1. Create recurring_costs table
2. Create notifications table
3. Create product_metrics table
4. Add vat_amount, original_cost, notes columns to sales table
5. Backfill product_metrics for existing products with days_listed calculation
"""
import sqlite3
from datetime import datetime
import sys


def migrate_database():
    """Add new tables and columns to the database"""
    conn = sqlite3.connect('crm.db')
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    try:
        print("Starting database migration...")

        # Check which tables/columns exist
        cursor.execute("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'")
        existing_tables = [row[0] for row in cursor.fetchall()]

        # 1. Create recurring_costs table
        if 'recurring_costs' not in existing_tables:
            print("Creating recurring_costs table...")
            cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE recurring_costs (
                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT,
                    amount FLOAT NOT NULL,
                    frequency VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'monthly',
                    category VARCHAR(100),
                    is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
                    start_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
                    end_date TIMESTAMP,
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                    updated_at TIMESTAMP
                )
            """)
            cursor.execute("CREATE INDEX idx_recurring_costs_is_active ON recurring_costs(is_active)")
            print("✓ Created recurring_costs table")
        else:
            print("✓ recurring_costs table already exists")

        # 2. Create notifications table
        if 'notifications' not in existing_tables:
            print("Creating notifications table...")
            cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE notifications (
                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
                    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
                    message TEXT NOT NULL,
                    read BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
                    metadata JSON,
                    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
                )
            """)
            cursor.execute("CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_type ON notifications(type)")
            cursor.execute("CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_read ON notifications(read)")
            cursor.execute("CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_created_at ON notifications(created_at)")
            print("✓ Created notifications table")
        else:
            print("✓ notifications table already exists")

        # 3. Create product_metrics table
        if 'product_metrics' not in existing_tables:
            print("Creating product_metrics table...")
            cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE product_metrics (
                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,
                    days_listed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                    view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                    times_price_reduced INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                    original_listing_price FLOAT,
                    optimal_price_suggestion FLOAT,
                    last_price_check TIMESTAMP,
                    updated_at TIMESTAMP,
                    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
                )
            """)
            cursor.execute("CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_product_metrics_product_id ON product_metrics(product_id)")
            print("✓ Created product_metrics table")

            # Backfill product_metrics for existing products
            print("Backfilling product_metrics for existing products...")
            cursor.execute("SELECT id, price, created_at FROM products")
            products = cursor.fetchall()

            for product_id, price, created_at in products:
                # Calculate days_listed
                if created_at:
                    created_date = datetime.fromisoformat(created_at.replace('Z', '+00:00'))
                    days_listed = (datetime.now() - created_date).days
                else:
                    days_listed = 0

                cursor.execute("""
                    INSERT INTO product_metrics (product_id, days_listed, original_listing_price, updated_at)
                    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
                """, (product_id, days_listed, price, datetime.now()))

            print(f"✓ Backfilled product_metrics for {len(products)} products")
        else:
            print("✓ product_metrics table already exists")

        # 4. Add new columns to sales table
        cursor.execute("PRAGMA table_info(sales)")
        sales_columns = [column[1] for column in cursor.fetchall()]

        if 'vat_amount' not in sales_columns:
            print("Adding vat_amount column to sales table...")
            cursor.execute("ALTER TABLE sales ADD COLUMN vat_amount FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0")
            print("✓ Added vat_amount column")
        else:
            print("✓ vat_amount column already exists")

        if 'original_cost' not in sales_columns:
            print("Adding original_cost column to sales table...")
            cursor.execute("ALTER TABLE sales ADD COLUMN original_cost FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0")
            print("✓ Added original_cost column")
        else:
            print("✓ original_cost column already exists")

        if 'notes' not in sales_columns:
            print("Adding notes column to sales table...")
            cursor.execute("ALTER TABLE sales ADD COLUMN notes TEXT")
            print("✓ Added notes column")
        else:
            print("✓ notes column already exists")

        # 5. Update net_profit for existing sales if needed
        print("Recalculating net_profit for existing sales...")
        cursor.execute("""
            UPDATE sales
            SET net_profit = sale_price - shipping_cost - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat_amount - original_cost
            WHERE net_profit IS NULL OR net_profit != (sale_price - shipping_cost - platform_fee - payment_fee - vat_amount - original_cost)
        """)
        updated_count = cursor.rowcount
        print(f"✓ Recalculated net_profit for {updated_count} sales")

        conn.commit()
        print("\n✅ Migration completed successfully!")
        print("\nNew tables created:")
        print("  - recurring_costs")
        print("  - notifications")
        print("  - product_metrics")
        print("\nNew columns added to sales:")
        print("  - vat_amount")
        print("  - original_cost")
        print("  - notes")

    except Exception as e:
        conn.rollback()
        print(f"\n❌ Migration failed: {str(e)}")
        sys.exit(1)

    finally:
        conn.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("=" * 60)
    print("CRM Database Migration - Enhanced Features")
    print("=" * 60)
    print()

    response = input("⚠️  This will modify your database. Continue? (yes/no): ")
    if response.lower() != 'yes':
        print("Migration cancelled.")
        sys.exit(0)

    print()
    migrate_database()
